gun.e: Guns were considered improvements of crossbows and catapults.

guy.d: An effigy of a man burned on a bonfire on the anniversary of the Gunpowder Plot (5th November).
guy.e: The dog's left foreleg was broken, poor little guy.

habit.d: An action performed on a regular basis.
habit.e: It’s become a habit of mine to have a cup of coffee after dinner.

hall.d: A manor house (originally because a magistrate's court was held in the hall of his mansion).
hall.e: The duke lived in a great hall overlooking the sea.

happily.d: In a happy or cheerful manner; with happiness.
happily.e: The children played happily in the rain.

headache.e: The clumsy filing system has been a huge headache.

heart.d: The organ in the chest that pumps blood through the body.
heart.e: Exercise is good for your heart.

heat.d: The output of a heating system.
heat.e: During the power outage we had no heat because the controls are electric.   Older folks like more heat than the young.

heavy.e: his eyes were heavy with sleep;  she was heavy with child

height.d: The highest point or maximum degree.
height.e: She's at the height of her career.

helpful.e: The shopkeeper was very helpful when we asked for directions.

hero.d: The current player, especially an hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: villain. Not to be confused with hero call.
hero.e: Let's discuss how to play if the hero has KK, and there's an ace on board.

hers.d: Used to refer to a thing or things belonging to or associated with a female person previously mentioned.

herself.d: She; an intensive repetition of the female subject, often used to indicate the exclusiveness of that person as the only satisfier of the predicate.

hide.d: To put (something) in a place where it will be harder to discover or out of sight.
hide.e: He hides his magazines under the bed.

hill.d: An elevated location smaller than a mountain.
hill.e: The park is sheltered from the wind by a hill to the east.

himself.d: He; used as an intensifier, often to emphasize that the referent is the exclusive participant in the predicate

hit.d: To manage to touch (a target) in the right place.

hockey.d: A variation of hockey, such as roller hockey, street hockey, shinny, or ball hockey.
hockey.e: India has a proud history in field hockey.

hold.d: To grasp, carry, or support with your hands or arms; to keep in a specified position.
hold.e: Can I have a hold of the baby?

hole.d: A hollow place or cavity; an excavation; a pit; an opening in or through a solid body, a fabric, etc.; a perforation; a rent; a fissure.
hole.e: There’s a hole in my shoe.  Her stocking has a hole in it.

huge.d: Distinctly interesting, significant, important, likeable, well regarded.
huge.e: Our next album is going to be huge!  In our league our coach is huge!

human.d: Having the nature or attributes of a human being.
human.e: To err is human; to forgive, divine.

hurt.d: To cause (a creature) physical pain and/or injury.
hurt.e: If anybody hurts my little brother, I will get upset.

ideal.d: Teaching or relating to the doctrine of idealism.

identify.d: To disclose the identity of someone.
identify.e: The Associated Press will not identify the suspect of the crime because he is a juvenile.

ill.d: Extremely bad (bad enough to make one ill). Generally used indirectly with to be.

illness.d: An instance of a disease or poor health.
illness.e: Her grandmother had passed away after a long illness.

image.d: A file that contains all information needed to produce a live working copy. (See disk image and image copy.)
image.e: Most game console emulators do not come with any ROM images for copyright reasons.

immediately.d: In an immediate manner; instantly or without delay.
immediately.e: I hope we can begin immediately.

impossible.d: Not possible; not able to be done or happen.
impossible.e: It is difficult, if not impossible, to memorize 20,000 consecutive numbers.

included.d: To bring into a group, class, set, or total as a (new) part or member.
included.e: I will purchase the vacation package if you will include car rental.

including.d: Such as, among which; introducing one or more parts of the group or topic just mentioned.
including.e: All you have to do is to fill in the details, including your name and address and the amount you wish to give.

increase.d: To become more nearly full; to show more of the surface; to wax.

incredible.d: Too implausible to be credible; beyond belief; unbelievable.
incredible.e: He was so wrapped up in watching the incredible special effects that he couldn't keep track of the story.

independent.d: Not affiliated with any political party.

individual.d: Relating to a single person or thing as opposed to more than one.
individual.e: As we can't print them all together, the individual pages will have to be printed one by one.

industry.d: The tendency to work persistently. Diligence.
industry.e: Over the years, their industry and business sense made them wealthy.

informal.d: Not in accord with the usual regulations.

injury.d: Damage to the body of a human or animal.
injury.e: The passenger sustained a severe injury in the car accident.

insect.d: An arthropod in the class Insecta, characterized by six legs, up to four wings, and a chitinous exoskeleton.
insect.e: Our shed has several insect infestions, including ants, yellowjackets, and wasps.

inside.d: Originating from, arranged by, or being someone inside an organisation.
inside.e: The reporter had received inside information about the forthcoming takeover.

instead.d: In the place of something (usually mentioned earlier); as a substitute or alternative.
instead.e: I was going to go shopping, but I went dancing instead.

instruction.d: The act of instructing, teaching, or furnishing with information or knowledge.
instruction.e: Instruction will be provided on how to handle difficult customers.

instructor.e: The swimming instructor taught the children basic strokes.

instrument.d: A device used to produce music.
instrument.e: The violinist was a master of her instrument.

intelligent.d: Having the same level of brain power as mankind.

international.d: Participated in by two or more nations

introduction.d: The act or process of introducing.
introduction.e: the introduction of a new product into the market

invent.d: To design a new process or mechanism.
invent.e: After weeks of hard work, I invented a new way to alphabetize matchbooks.

invention.d: A small, self-contained composition, particularly those in J.S. Bach’s Two- and Three-part Inventions.
invention.e: I particularly like the inventions in C-minor.

invitation.d: A document or verbal message conveying an invitation.
invitation.e: We need to print off fifty invitations for the party.

invite.d: To ask for the presence or participation of someone or something.
invite.e: We invited our friends round for dinner.

involve.d: To engage (someone) to participate in a task.
involve.e: By getting involved in her local community, Mary met lots of people and also helped make it a nicer place to live.

item.d: A matter for discussion in an agenda.
item.e: The first item for discussion is the budget for next year's picnic.

itself.d: It; used to intensify the subject, especially to emphasize that it is the only participant in the predicate
itself.e: The door itself is quite heavy.

jam.d: A sweet mixture of fruit boiled with sugar and allowed to congeal. Often spread on bread or toast or used in jam tarts.
jam.e: A traffic jam caused us to miss the game's first period.

jazz.d: A musical art form rooted in West African cultural and musical expression and in the African American blues tradition, with diverse influences over time, commonly characterized by blue notes, syncopation, swing, call and response, polyrhythms and improvisation.
jazz.e: What is all this jazz lying around?

jewellery.d: Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.
jewellery.e: She had more jewellery ornamented about her than any three ladies needed.

joke.d: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.

journalist.d: The keeper of a personal journal, who writes in it regularly.
journalist.e: The journalist interviewed the politician about the new policy.

jump.d: To push yourself off the ground and into the air using your legs.
jump.e: The cat jumped onto the table.

kid.e: The kids are playing in the garden.

kill.d: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.

king.d: A powerful or majorly influential person.
king.e: Howard Stern styled himself as the "king of all media".

knee.d: The joint between the thigh and the lower leg.
knee.e: He hurt his knee while playing football.

knife.d: A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.

knock.d: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.
knock.e: Knock on the door and find out if they’re home.

knowledge.d: Familiarity or understanding of a particular skill, branch of learning etc.
knowledge.e: A secretary should have a good knowledge of shorthand.

lab.e: I dropped my mixture while doing my lab, so I had to start over.

lady.d: A woman of breeding or higher class, a woman of authority.
lady.e: "I would like the dining room to be fully set by tonight; would you do so?" "Yes, my lady".

lake.d: A small stream of running water; a channel for water; a drain.
lake.e: For example, the name of a lake prepared by extending the aluminum salt prepared from FD&C Blue No. 1 upon the substratum would be FD&C Blue No. 1--Aluminum Lake.

lamp.d: A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.

laptop.e: She bought a new laptop for her college coursework.

laughter.d: The sound of laughing, produced by air so expelled; any similar sound.
laughter.e: Their loud laughter betrayed their presence.

law.d: The profession that deals with such rules (as lawyers, judges, police officers, etc).
law.e: He is studying for a career in law.

lawyer.d: A professional person qualified (as by a law degree or bar exam) and authorized to practice law, i.e. represent parties in lawsuits or trials and give legal advice.
lawyer.e: A lawyer's time and advice are his stock in trade. - aphorism often credited to Abraham Lincoln, but without attestation

lazy.d: Employing lazy evaluation; not calculating results until they are immediately required.

leader.d: Any person that leads or directs
leader.e: She was chosen as the team leader for the project.

learning.d: An act in which something is learned.
learning.e: The department head was also a scholar of great learning.

lecture.d: To teach (somebody) by giving a speech on a given topic.
lecture.e: The professor lectured to two classes this morning.

lemon.e: He didn’t realise until he’d paid for it that the car was a lemon.

lend.d: To allow to be used by someone temporarily, on condition that it or its equivalent will be returned.
lend.e: I lent her 10 euros to pay for the train tickets, and she paid me back the next day.

lifestyle.d: A style of living that reflects the attitudes and values of a person or group.
lifestyle.e: A healthy lifestyle includes regular exercise and a balanced diet.

lift.e: She lifts twice a week at the gym.

likely.d: Probable; having a greater-than-even chance of occurring
likely.e: Rain is likely later this afternoon.

link.e: Stop linking those unfunny comics all the time!

listener.d: Someone who listens, especially to a speech or a broadcast.
listener.e: A good listener pays attention without interrupting.

lock.e: Remember to lock the door when you leave.

lorry.d: A motor vehicle for transporting goods, and in some cases people; a truck.

lost.d: Having wandered from, or unable to find, the way.
lost.e: The children were soon lost in the forest.

loud.e: Turn that music down; it's too loud.

loudly.d: In a loud manner; at a high volume.
loudly.e: He spoke loudly so that his brother could hear him from across the street.

lovely.d: Beautiful; charming; very pleasing in form, looks, tone, or manner.
lovely.e: It's a lovely day and the sun is shining.

low.d: Below the usual or expected level.
low.e: Prices are low at this shop.

luck.d: Something that happens to someone by chance, a chance occurrence, especially a favourable one.
luck.e: Gilbert had some bad luck yesterday — he got pick-pocketed and lost fifty dollars.

lucky.d: Producing, or resulting in, good fortune

mail.d: To contact (a person) by electronic mail.
mail.e: I need to mail my tutor about the deadline.

major.e: the major part of the assembly

male.d: Of or relating to men or boys.
male.e: The doctor asked if the patient was male or female.

manage.e: He managed to climb the tower.

manager.d: A person whose job is to manage something, such as a business, a restaurant, or a sports team.
manager.e: a file manager; a task manager; Program Manager

manner.e: It is in a manner done already.

mark.d: To leave a mark (often an undesirable or unwanted one) on (something).
mark.e: See where this pencil has marked the paper.

marry.d: To take as husband or wife.
marry.e: In some cultures, it is acceptable for an uncle to marry his niece.

material.e: Don't let material concerns get in the way of living a happy life.

mathematics.d: A person's ability to count, calculate, and use different systems of mathematics at differing levels.

maths.d: A person's ability to count, calculate, and use different systems of mathematics at differing levels.

matter.d: A condition, subject or affair, especially one of concern.

media.d: Means and institutions for publishing and broadcasting information.
media.e: As a result of the rise of, first, television news and entertainment media and, second, web-based media, traditional print-based media has declined in popularity.

medical.d: Of or pertaining to the practice of medicine.
medical.e: Do you have any medical experience?

medicine.d: The study of the cause, diagnosis, prognosis and treatment of disease or illness.
medicine.e: She's studying medicine at university because she wants to be a doctor in the future.

memory.d: The ability of the brain to record information or impressions with the facility of recalling them later at will.
memory.e: Memory is a facility common to all animals.

mention.d: A speaking or notice of anything, usually in a brief or cursory manner. Used especially in the phrase make mention of.
mention.e: Did she mention the time of the meeting?

metal.d: (heading) Chemical elements or alloys, and the mines where their ores come from.
metal.e: We have American Airlines tickets, but it's on British Airways metal.

method.d: A particular procedure for accomplishing or approaching something, especially a systematic or established one.
method.e: We need a new method to solve this problem.

middle.d: Located in the middle; in between.

might.d: Past tense of 'may'; used to express possibility, especially a smaller chance.
might.e: She might come to the party.

mind.d: The part of a person that thinks, reasons, feels, and remembers; the intellect.
mind.e: You should mind your own business.

mirror.d: A smooth surface, usually made of glass with reflective material painted on the underside, that reflects light so as to give an image of what is in front of it.
mirror.e: I had a look in the mirror to see if the blood had come off my face.

missing.d: Not able to be located; gone, misplaced.

mobile.d: Pertaining to or by agency of mobile phones.

monkey.d: Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
monkey.e: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

moon.d: The natural satellite of the earth, visible at night by reflected sunlight.
moon.e: The moon was full and bright last night.

mostly.e: They're mostly good people, although they have made a few mistakes.

motorcycle.d: An open-seated motor vehicle with handlebars instead of a steering wheel, and having two (or sometimes three) wheels.
motorcycle.e: He rides his motorcycle to work every day.

movement.d: Physical motion between points in space.
movement.e: I saw a movement in that grass on the hill.

musical.d: Of, belonging or relating to music, or to its performance or notation.

musician.d: A composer, conductor, or performer of music; specifically, a person who sings and/or plays a musical instrument as a hobby, an occupation, or a profession.
musician.e: The musician played a beautiful raga on the sitar.

myself.d: Me (as the object of a verb or preposition).

narrow.d: Of small width in relation to length; not wide.
narrow.e: The Republicans won by a narrow majority.

national.e: The diplomats were advised not to interact with any foreign nationals except on official duty.

nature.d: The natural world; that which consists of all things unaffected by or predating human technology, production, and design. (Compare ecosystem.)

nearly.e: He left a nearly full beer on the bar.

necessary.d: Required, essential, whether logically inescapable or needed in order to achieve a desired result or avoid some penalty.
necessary.e: Although I wished to think that all was false, it was yet necessary that I, who thus thought, must in some sense exist.

neck.d: The part of the body connecting the head and the trunk found in humans and some animals.

neither.e: I've tried on both shirts, but neither fits properly.

nervous.e: She felt nervous before her first job interview.

network.d: A fabric or structure of fibrous elements attached to each other at regular intervals.
network.e: A network of roads crisscrossed the country.

noise.d: Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.
noise.e: He knew that it was trash day, when the garbage collectors made all the noise.

noisy.d: Making a noise, especially a loud unpleasant sound

none.e: None of this meat tastes right.

normal.d: According to norms or rules or to a regular pattern.
normal.e: Organize the data into third normal form.

normally.d: Under normal conditions or circumstances; usually; most of the time
normally.e: Normally, I eat breakfast at 6am, but today, I got up late and didn't eat until 9.

notice.d: To become aware of; to observe.
notice.e: Did you notice the flowers in her yard?

novel.d: A work of prose fiction, longer than a novella.
novel.e: She finished reading the novel in two days.

nowhere.d: Not in or to any place; not anywhere.
nowhere.e: There was nowhere to sit on the crowded bus.

nut.d: A hard-shelled fruit of certain plants, containing an edible kernel.

ocean.e: The island is surrounded by ocean

offer.d: To present in words; to proffer; to make a proposal of; to suggest.

officer.d: One who has a position of authority in a hierarchical organization, especially in military, police or government organizations.

oil.d: A thick liquid used for cooking, fuel, or lubrication.
oil.e: Heat some oil in the pan before adding the onions.

onto.d: Moving to a position on the surface of something.
onto.e: The cat jumped onto the table.

opportunity.d: A chance for advancement, progress or profit.
opportunity.e: The world is full of opportunities and it's up to me to see them and pursue them.

option.d: One of a set of choices that can be made.

ordinary.d: Being part of the natural order of things; normal, customary, routine.
ordinary.e: On an ordinary day I wake up at nine o'clock, work for six hours, and then go to the gym.

organization.e: This painting shows little organization at first glance, but little by little the structure becomes clear.

organize.d: To band together into a group or union that can bargain and act collectively; to unionize.
organize.e: the workers decided to organize; their next task was to organize the workers at the steel mill

original.d: First in a series or copies/versions
original.e: The original manuscript contained spelling errors which were fixed in later versions.

ourselves.d: We; intensifies the subject as the group including the speaker, especially to indicate that no one else satisfies the predicate.

oven.d: A chamber used for baking or heating.
oven.e: She baked cookies in the oven for the school fair.

owner.e: The police recovered the stolen car and returned it to its owner.

pain.d: An ache or bodily suffering, or an instance of this; an unpleasant sensation, resulting from a derangement of functions, disease, or injury by violence; hurt.
pain.e: I had to stop running when I started getting pains in my feet.

painter.e: The painter created a stunning landscape of the Himalayas.

palace.d: Official residence of a head of state or other dignitary, especially in a monarchical or imperial governmental system.
palace.e: The City Palace in Jaipur is a major tourist attraction.

pants.d: (Manchester) An outer garment that covers the body from the waist downwards, covering each leg separately, usually as far as the ankles; trousers.
pants.e: The film was a load [or pile] of pants.

parking.d: The action of the verb to park.
parking.e: Parking in central London can be very difficult.

particular.d: Specialised; characteristic of a specific person or thing.
particular.e: I don't appreciate your particular brand of cynicism.

pass.d: A password (especially one for a restricted-access website).

passenger.d: One who rides or travels in a vehicle, but who does not operate it and is not a member of the crew.
passenger.e: Somebody in a team who does not do their fair share of the work.

patient.d: Constant in pursuit or exertion; persevering; calmly diligent.

peace.d: A state free of oppressive and unpleasant thoughts and emotions.
peace.e: The safety equipment will give me some peace of mind.

penny.d: In the United Kingdom and Ireland, a copper coin worth 1/240 of a pound sterling or Irish pound before decimalisation. Abbreviation: d.

per.d: To each, in each (used in expressing ratios of units).

per cent.d: A percentage, a proportion (especially per hundred).
per cent.e: only a small percent attain the top ranks

perform.d: To do (something) in front of an audience, such as acting or music, often in order to entertain.
perform.e: She will perform in the play.

perhaps.d: Used to express possibility or uncertainty.
perhaps.e: Perhaps we could meet next week.

permission.d: Flags or access control lists pertaining to a file that dictate who can access it, and how.
permission.e: I used the "chmod" command to change the file's permission.

personality.d: A set of non-physical psychological and social qualities that make a person (or thing) distinct from another.
personality.e: The president has a unique personality.

pet.d: An animal kept as a companion.

physical.e: How long has it been since your last physical?

physics.d: The physical aspects of a phenomenon or a system, especially those studied scientifically.
physics.e: The physics of car crashes would not let Tom Cruise walk away like that.

pick.d: To grasp and pull with the fingers or fingernails.

pilot.d: A person who steers a ship, a helmsman.
pilot.e: We would like to run a pilot in your facility before rolling out the program citywide.

planet.d: In phrases such as the planet, this planet, sometimes refers to the Earth.
planet.e: Mars is the fourth planet from the sun.

plastic.d: A synthetic, solid, hydrocarbon-based polymer, whether thermoplastic or thermosetting.
plastic.e: We should avoid using single-use plastic bags.

plate.d: A slightly curved but almost flat dish from which food is served or eaten.
plate.e: I filled my plate from the bountiful table.

platform.d: A raised stage from which speeches are made and on which musical and other performances are made.
platform.e: This new talk show will give a platform to everyday men and women.

pleased.d: Feeling or showing pleasure and satisfaction.

pocket.d: A bag stitched to an item of clothing, used for carrying small items.
pocket.e: I paid for it out of my own pocket.

polite.e: It's not polite to use a mobile phone in a restaurant.

pollution.e: Air pollution is a serious problem in many Indian cities.

pop.d: A loud, sharp sound as of a cork coming out of a bottle.
pop.e: Listen to the pop of a champagne cork.

population.d: The people living within a political or geographical boundary.
population.e: The population of New Jersey will not stand for this!

position.e: My position on this issue is unchanged.

possession.d: A territory under the rule of another country.
possession.e: Réunion is the largest of France's overseas possessions.

possibility.e: There is a possibility of rain this evening.

poster.d: An advertisement to be posted on a pole, wall etc. to advertise something.
poster.e: I saw a poster for the film on the side of a bus.

power.d: To provide power for (a mechanical or electronic device).
power.e: This CD player is powered by batteries.

predict.d: To direct a ranged weapon against a target by means of a predictor.
predict.e: Scientists try to predict earthquakes before they happen.

president.d: The head of state of a republic.
president.e: The vast majority of presidents have been male.

prevent.d: To stop (an outcome); to keep from (doing something).
prevent.e: I brush my teeth regularly to prevent them from turning yellow.

print.d: To produce a microchip (an integrated circuit) in a process resembling the printing of an image.
print.e: The circuitry is printed onto the semiconductor surface.

printer.d: The operator of a printing press, or the owner of a printing business.
printer.e: The printer ran out of ink during the exam.

prison.d: A place or institution of confinement, especially of long-term confinement for those convicted of serious crimes or otherwise considered undesirable by the government.
prison.e: The cold stone walls of the prison had stood for over a century.

prize.d: That which is taken from another; something captured; a thing seized by force, stratagem, or superior power.
prize.e: I paid a high price for my folly.

process.d: To retrieve, store, classify, manipulate, transmit etc. (data, signals, etc.), especially using computer techniques.
process.e: We have processed the data using our proven techniques, and have come to the following conclusions.

produce.d: To extend an area, or lengthen a line.
produce.e: to produce a side of a triangle

professional.d: Of, pertaining to, or in accordance with the (usually high) standards of a profession.
professional.e: She is a professional dancer who has performed all over the world.

professor.d: The most senior rank for an academic at a university or similar institution, informally also known as "full professor." Abbreviated Prof.
professor.e: The professor gave an engaging lecture on Indian history.

profile.d: The outermost shape, view, or edge of an object.
profile.e: His fingers traced the profile of the handle.

program.d: To enter a program or other instructions into (a computer or other electronic device) to instruct it to do a particular task.
program.e: He programmed the DVR to record his favorite show.

progress.d: To move, go, or proceed forward; to advance.

promise.d: To give grounds for expectation, especially of something good.

pronounce.d: To declare formally, officially or ceremoniously.
pronounce.e: I hereby pronounce you man and wife.

protect.d: To keep safe; to defend; to guard; to prevent harm coming to.
protect.e: Condoms are designed to protect against sexually-transmitted diseases.

provide.d: To act to prepare for something.

pub.d: A public house where beverages, primarily alcoholic, may be bought and consumed, also providing food and sometimes entertainment such as live music or television.

public.d: The people in general, regardless of membership of any particular group.
public.e: Members of the public may not proceed beyond this point.

publish.d: To issue (something, such as printed work) for distribution and/or sale.
publish.e: Most of the sketches Faulkner published in 1925 appeared in the Sunday magazine section of the New Orleans Times-Picayune.

pull.d: Any device meant to be pulled, as a lever, knob, handle, or rope

purpose.d: The reason for which something is done, or the reason it is done in a particular way.
purpose.e: The purpose of turning off the lights overnight is to save energy.

push.d: To apply a force to (an object) such that it moves away from the person or thing applying the force.
push.e: In his anger he pushed me against the wall and threatened me.

quality.d: High social position. (See also the quality.)
quality.e: A peasant is not allowed to fall in love with a lady of quality.

quantity.e: Olive oil can be used practically in any quantity.

queen.d: A female monarch. Example: Queen Victoria.

quietly.e: The students worked quietly during the exam.

railway.d: A transport system using rails used to move passengers or goods.
railway.e: The Indian railway network is one of the largest in the world.

raise.e: We need to raise the motivation level in the company.

rate.d: To assign or be assigned a particular rank or level.
rate.e: She is rated fourth in the country.

rather.d: Used to specify a choice or preference; preferably, in preference to. (Now usually followed by than)
rather.e: I'd like this one rather than the other one.

reach.d: The ability to reach or touch with the person, a limb, or something held or thrown.
reach.e: The fruit is beyond my reach.

react.d: To act or perform a second time; to do over again; to reenact.
react.e: Every body reacts on the body that impels it from its natural state.

realize.d: To become aware of (a fact or situation, especially of something that has been true for a long time).
realize.e: He realized that he had left his umbrella on the train.

receive.d: To take, as something that is offered, given, committed, sent, paid, etc.; to accept; to be given something.
receive.e: She received many presents for her birthday.

recent.d: Having done something a short while ago that distinguishes them as what they are called.
recent.e: I met three recent graduates at the conference.

reception.d: The act or ability to receive radio or similar signals.
reception.e: The new system provides exceptional quality of the reception signal.

recipe.d: A set of conditions and parameters of an industrial process to obtain a given result.

recognize.d: To match (something or someone which one currently perceives) to a memory of some previous encounter with the same person or thing.

worst.e: It is bad manners to talk with your mouth full.

wow.d: An indication of excitement, surprise, astonishment, or pleasure.

anxious.d: Having a feeling of anxiety or disquietude; extremely concerned, especially about something that will happen in the future or that is unknown.
anxious.e: I could tell she was anxious as she was biting her nails.

artistic.e: The artistic designs on the temple walls are breathtaking.

aspect.d: Any specific feature, part, or element of something.

associated.d: (of a person or thing) connected with something or another person.

association.e: She is a member of the teachers' association.

assume.e: Mr. Jones will assume the position of a lifeguard until a proper replacement is found.

Fancy going for a drink after work?
You bet I do!

commonly.e: Mangoes are commonly found in Indian markets during summer.

currency.d: Money or other items used to facilitate transactions.

financial.d: Having dues and fees paid up to date for a club or society.

shy.e: He is very shy with strangers.

sight.d: (in the singular) The ability to see.
sight.e: He is losing his sight and now can barely read.

signal.d: To communicate with (a person or system) by a signal.
signal.e: Seeing the flames, he ran to the control room and signalled headquarters.

silent.d: With the sound turned off; usually on silent or in silent mode.

silly.d: Laughable or amusing through foolishness or a foolish appearance.
silly.e: The children laughed at the silly joke.

similarity.e: Hardly is there a similarity detected between two or three facts, than men hasten to extend it to all. — Sir W. Hamilton.

simply.d: In a straightforward or plain manner; just; merely.
simply.e: I simply don't have the time.

sink.d: (heading, physical) To move or be moved into something.
sink.e: I have sunk thousands of pounds into this project.

slice.d: To cut with an edge utilizing a drawing motion.
slice.e: The knife left sliced his arm.

slightly.e: He was slightly built, but tall.

smart.d: Cleverly shrewd and humorous in a way that may be rude and disrespectful.

smooth.d: Without difficulty, problems, or unexpected consequences or incidents.
smooth.e: We hope for a smooth transition to the new system.

software.d: Encoded computer instructions, usually modifiable (unless stored in some form of unalterable memory such as ROM).
software.e: The company develops software for banking and finance.

soil.d: A mixture of mineral particles and organic material, used to support plant growth.

solid.d: Lacking holes, hollows or admixtures of other materials.

southern.d: Of or pertaining to a southern region, especially Southern Europe or the southern United States.

specifically.d: In a specific manner, applying to or naming a particular thing or things, expressly, explicitly
specifically.e: The rule applies specifically to new employees.

spicy.d: Of, pertaining to, or containing spice.

spirit.d: The soul of a person or other creature. What moves through experience into self-definition as souls purpose.
spirit.e: A wandering spirit haunts the island.

spot.d: To place an object at a location indicated by a spot. Notably in billiards or snooker.
spot.e: The referee had to spot the pink on the blue spot.

spread.d: To stretch out, open out (a material etc.) so that it more fully covers a given area of space.
spread.e: He spread his newspaper on the table.

stadium.d: A venue where sporting events are held.
stadium.e: The new stadium can seat over fifty thousand people.

staff.d: (plural staffs or staves) A long, straight, thick wooden rod or stick, especially one used to assist in walking.
staff.e: The company employed 10 new members of staff this month.

standard.e: standard works in history; standard authors

statistic.d: A person, or personal event, reduced to being an item of statistical information.
statistic.e: By dying from an overdose, he became just another statistic.

statue.d: A three-dimensional work of art, usually representing a person or animal, usually created by sculpting, carving, molding, or casting.

stranger.d: A person whom one does not know; a person who is neither a friend nor an acquaintance.
stranger.e: That gentleman is a stranger to me.  Children are taught not to talk to strangers.

strength.d: The quality or degree of being strong.
strength.e: It requires great strength to lift heavy objects.

string.d: A building, wing or dependency set apart and adapted for lodging and feeding (and training) animals with hoofs, especially horses.
string.e: There were stalls for fourteen horses in the squire's stables.

strongly.d: In a strong or powerful manner.
strongly.e: In the third race, Renowned Blaze finished strongly to win, paying sixteen dollars.

studio.d: An artist’s or photographer’s workshop or the room in which an artist works.
studio.e: His studio was cramped when he began as an artist.

stuff.d: To fill a space with (something) in a compressed manner.
stuff.e: He stuffed his clothes into the closet and shut the door.

substance.e: Some textile fabrics have little substance.

successfully.d: In a successful manner; with success; without failing.

sudden.d: Happening quickly and with little or no warning.
sudden.e: The sudden drop in temperature left everyone cold and confused.

suffer.e: Is anyone here afraid of suffering hardship?

suitable.d: Having sufficient or the required properties for a certain purpose or task; appropriate to a certain occasion.
suitable.e: This book is suitable for beginners learning English.

summarize.e: Jim was asked to summarize the document by Wednesday.

summary.d: An abstract or a condensed presentation of the substance of a body of material.

supply.e: A supply of good drinking water is essential.

supporter.d: A person who gives support to someone or something.
supporter.e: The cricket team has millions of supporters across India.

surely.d: Without doubt; certainly; used to express confidence.

surface.d: The overside or up-side of a flat object such as a table, or of a liquid.
surface.e: On the surface, the spy looked like a typical businessman.

survive.d: To live past a life-threatening event.
survive.e: He did not survive the accident.

switch.d: To change (something) to the specified state using a switch.

symptom.d: A perceived change in some function, sensation or appearance of a person that indicates a disease or disorder, such as fever, headache or rash.
symptom.e: Fever and cough are common symptoms of the flu.

tail.d: The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to its posterior and near the anus.
tail.e: Most primates have a tail and fangs.

talent.d: People of talent, viewed collectively; a talented person.
talent.e: The director searched their talent pool to fill the new opening.

talented.d: Endowed with one or more talents.
talented.e: The talented singer won the reality show.

tape.d: Flexible material in a roll with a sticky surface on one or both sides; adhesive tape.
tape.e: Hand me some tape. I need to fix a tear in this paper.

tax.d: To impose and collect a tax from (a person or company).
tax.e: Some think to tax the wealthy is the fairest.

technical.e: The performance showed technical virtuosity, but lacked inspiration.

technique.d: Practical ability in some given field or practice, often as opposed to creativity or imaginative skill.
technique.e: The artist uses a unique painting technique.

tend.d: To accompany as an assistant or protector; to care for the wants of; to look after; to watch; to guard.

tent.d: A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering people from the weather.
tent.e: We were camping in a three-man tent.

theirs.d: Used to refer to a thing or things belonging to or associated with two or more people previously mentioned.

theme.d: A subject of a talk or an artistic piece; a topic.

theory.d: A field of study attempting to exhaustively describe a particular class of constructs.
theory.e: Knot theory classifies the mappings of a circle into 3-space.

therefore.d: Consequently, by or in consequence of that or this cause; referring to something previously stated.
therefore.e: Traditional values will always have a place, therefore they will never lose relevance.

though.e: Though it’s risky, it’s worth taking the chance.

throat.d: The front part of the neck.
throat.e: The wild pitch bounced and hit the catcher in the throat.

tin.d: A metal pan used for baking, roasting, etc.

tiny.d: Extremely small in size or amount.
tiny.e: The baby had tiny little fingers.

toe.d: Each of the five digits on the end of the foot.

tongue.d: The flexible muscular organ in the mouth that is used to move food around, for tasting and that is moved into various positions to modify the flow of air from the lungs in order to produce different sounds in speech.

total.d: An amount obtained by the addition of smaller amounts.
total.e: A total of £145 was raised by the bring-and-buy stall.

totally.d: To the fullest extent or degree.
totally.e: They've totally demolished the bank on Main Street.

tourism.d: The act of visiting another region or jurisdiction for a particular purpose.
tourism.e: libel tourism; suicide tourism; sex tourism

towards.e: What are your feelings toward him?

trade.d: To be traded at a certain price or under certain conditions.

translate.d: To entrance, to cause to lose recollection or sense.
translate.e: William was translated by the blow to the head he received, being unable to speak for the next few minutes.

translation.d: The act of translating, in its various senses:
translation.e: The translation of the book from Hindi to English was well done.

various.d: Having a broad range (of different elements).

version.d: A change of form, direction, etc.; transformation; conversion.
version.e: External cephalic version is a process by which a breech baby can sometimes be turned from buttocks or foot first to head first.

victim.d: A living being which is slain and offered as a sacrifice, usually in a religious rite.
victim.e: the youngest victims of the brutal war

viewer.d: Any optical device used to view photographic slides.
viewer.e: The television show attracted millions of viewers.

violent.e: A violent wind ripped the branch from the tree.

volunteer.d: To do or offer to do something voluntarily.
volunteer.e: to volunteer for doing the dishes

vote.d: To choose or grant by means of a vote, or by general consent
vote.e: The depository may vote shares on behalf of investors who have not submitted instruction to the bank.

warn.d: To caution or admonish (someone) against unwise or unacceptable behaviour.
warn.e: Don't let me catch you running in the corridor again, I warn you.

warning.d: The action of the verb warn; an instance of warning someone.
warning.e: The boss gave him a warning that he would be fired if he did not desist from his behaviour.

waste.d: To squander (money or resources) uselessly; to spend (time) idly.
waste.e: We wasted millions of dollars and several years on that project.

weapon.d: An instrument of attack or defense in combat or hunting, e.g. most guns, missiles, or swords.
weapon.e: The club that is now mostly used for golf was once a common weapon.

weigh.d: Often with "out", to measure a certain amount of something by its weight, e.g. for sale.
weigh.e: He weighed out two kilos of oranges for a client.

western.d: Of, facing, situated in, or related to the west.

whatever.e: Whatever he does, he will still lose the game.

whenever.d: At the (single) time that, no matter when.
whenever.e: Tomorrow I'll get up whenever the sun rises.

whether.d: Used to introduce an indirect interrogative question that consists of multiple alternative possibilities (usually with correlative or).
whether.e: He chose the correct answer, but I don't know whether it was by luck or (whether it was) by skill.

wing.d: A part of something that is lesser in size than the main body, such as an extension from the main building.
wing.e: the west wing of the hospital

within.d: In the inner part, spatially; physically inside.

wonder.d: To ponder; to feel doubt and curiosity; to query in the mind.
wonder.e: He wondered whether penguins could fly. She had wondered this herself sometimes.

wool.d: A cloth or yarn made from the wool of sheep.
wool.e: She knitted a woollen scarf for the winter.

worldwide.e: A large meteorite impact would cause worldwide extinction of life.

worth.d: Making a fair equivalent of, repaying or compensating.
worth.e: This job is hardly worth the effort.

written.d: Of, relating, or characteristic of writing (i.e., of that which has been written).
written.e: I can speak Japanese fairly well, but I have no understanding whatsoever of written Japanese.

yard.d: A small, usually uncultivated area adjoining or (now especially) within the precincts of a house or other building.
yard.e: I need to hedge a yard of yen.

youth.d: The quality or state of being young.
youth.e: Her youth and beauty attracted him to her.

abandon.d: To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.
abandon.e: Many baby girls have been abandoned on the streets of Beijing.

absolute.d: As measured using an absolute value.

acceptable.d: Worthy, decent, sure of being accepted or received with at least moderate pleasure
acceptable.e: We need to find an acceptable present for Jeff.

accompany.d: To perform an accompanying part next to (another instrument or musician).
accompany.e: I will accompany her on the oboe.

accurate.d: Deviating only slightly or within acceptable limits.
accurate.e: My horoscopes I read last week were surprisingly accurate.

accuse.d: To find fault with, blame, censure
accuse.e: For the U.S. President to be impeached, he must be accused of a high crime or misdemeanor.

acknowledge.d: To be grateful of (e.g. a benefit or a favour)

acquire.d: To gain, usually by one's own exertions; to get as one's own

actual.d: Existing in reality, not just potentially; really acted or acting; occurring in fact.
actual.e: The actual government expenses dramatically exceed the budget.

adapt.d: To make oneself comfortable to a new thing.
adapt.e: They could not adapt to the new climate and so perished.

additional.e: The teacher provided additional notes for revision.

administration.d: An arrangement whereby an insolvent company can continue trading under supervision.
administration.e: The company went into voluntary administration last week.

adopt.d: To take or receive as one's own what is not so naturally.
adopt.e: He adopted a new look in order to fit in with his new workmates.

advance.d: Completed before necessary or a milestone event.
advance.e: He made an advance payment on the prior shipment to show good faith.

affair.d: Any proceeding or action which it is wished to refer to or characterize vaguely.
affair.e: an affair of honor, a duel;  an affair of love, an intrigue

afterwards.d: (temporal location) At a later or succeeding time.
afterwards.e: We had lunch and went for a walk afterwards.

agency.d: The capacity of individuals to act independently and to make their own free choices.

agenda.d: A notebook used to organize and maintain such plans or lists, an agenda book, an agenda planner.
agenda.e: The first item on the agenda is the budget review.

aggressive.d: Characterized by aggression; unjustly attacking; prone to behave in a way that involves attacking or arguing.
aggressive.e: an aggressive policy, war, person, nation

aid.d: An aide-de-camp, so called by abbreviation.
aid.e: The incompetent general's brilliant aid often made priceless suggestions.

aircraft.d: A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift
aircraft.e: The aircraft landed safely despite the strong winds.

alter.d: To change the form or structure of.
alter.e: The tailor altered the dress to fit her perfectly.

anger.d: A strong feeling of displeasure, hostility or antagonism towards someone or something, usually combined with an urge to harm.
anger.e: You need to control your anger.

blame.d: Culpability for something negative or undesirable.
blame.e: The blame for starting the fire lies with the arsonist.

blind.d: (of a person or animal) Unable to see, due to physiological or neurological factors.
blind.e: Authors are blind to their own defects.

bond.d: Evidence of a long-term debt, by which the bond issuer (the borrower) is obliged to pay interest when due, and repay the principal at maturity, as specified on the face of the bond certificate. The rights of the holder are specified in the bond indenture, which contains the legal terms and conditions under which the bond was issued. Bonds are available in two forms: registered bonds, and bearer bonds.
bond.e: Investors face a quandary. Cash offers a return of virtually zero in many developed countries; government-bond yields may have risen in recent weeks but they are still unattractive. Equities have suffered two big bear markets since 2000 and are wobbling again. It is hardly surprising that pension funds, insurers and endowments are searching for new sources of return.

breast.d: Either of the two organs on the front of a female human's chest, which contain the mammary glands; also the analogous organs in males.
breast.e: Tanya's breasts grew remarkably during pregnancy.

brief.e: Her reign was brief but spectacular.

broadcast.d: To appear as a performer, presenter, or speaker in a broadcast programme.
broadcast.e: The DJ was feeling nervous before his first national broadcast.

budget.d: The amount of money or resources earmarked for a particular institution, activity or time-frame.

bullet.d: A projectile, usually of metal, shot from a gun at high speed.
bullet.e: John's not going to any of his top schools; he got a bullet from the last of them yesterday.

bunch.d: A group of similar things, either growing together, or in a cluster or clump, usually fastened together.
bunch.e: a bunch of grapes;  a bunch of bananas;  a bunch of keys;  a bunch of yobs on a street corner

bush.d: A woody plant distinguished from a tree by its multiple stems and lower height, being usually less than six metres tall; a horticultural rather than strictly botanical category.

cable.d: (material) A long object used to make a physical connection.
cable.e: I tried to watch the movie last night but my cable was out.

calculate.d: To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.
calculate.e: Calculate the square root of 3 to 10 decimal places.

cancel.e: He cancelled his order on their website.

cancer.d: A disease in which the cells of a tissue undergo uncontrolled (and often rapid) proliferation.
cancer.e: Early detection of cancer greatly improves treatment outcomes.

capable.d: Able and efficient; having the ability needed for a specific task; having the disposition to do something; permitting or being susceptible to something.
capable.e: As everyone knew, he was capable of violence when roused.

capacity.d: The maximum amount that can be held
capacity.e: It was hauling a capacity load.

capture.d: To store (as in sounds or image) for later revisitation.
capture.e: She captured the details of the fresco in a series of photographs.

cast.d: The mould used to make cast objects.
cast.e: A plaster cast was made from his face.

cell.d: The minimal unit of a cellular automaton that can change state and has an associated behavior.
cell.e: The upper right cell always starts with the color green.

chairman.d: A person presiding over a meeting.
chairman.e: The chairman addressed the shareholders at the annual meeting.

characteristic.d: Being a distinguishing feature of a person or thing.
characteristic.e: The characteristic of a field, if non-zero, must be a prime number.

convert.d: To transform or change (something) into another form, substance, state, or product.
convert.e: A kettle converts water into steam.

convinced.d: In a state of believing, especially from evidence but not necessarily.
convinced.e: He was convinced he was a great singer, statements of others to the contrary.

core.d: The central part of fruit, containing the kernels or seeds.
core.e: the core of an apple or quince

corporate.d: Related to corporation that franchises rather than an individual franchise.
corporate.e: The one on Seventh Street is a corporate franchise.

council.d: A committee that leads or governs (e.g. city council, student council).
council.e: The city council approved the new park project.

county.d: A jail operated by a county government.
county.e: He can't come; he's up in the county for agg assault.

courage.d: The quality of being confident, not afraid or easily intimidated, but without being incautious or inconsiderate.
courage.e: It takes a lot of courage to be successful in business.

crash.d: To severely damage or destroy something by causing it to collide with something else.
crash.e: I'm sorry for crashing the bike into a wall. I'll pay for repairs.

creation.d: Something created such as an invention or artwork.
creation.e: I think the manufacturer was so ashamed of its creation that it didn't put its name on it!

creature.d: A living being; an animal or (sometimes derogatory) a human.
creature.e: He's a creature of habit.   insects and other creatures

crew.d: A pen for livestock such as chickens or pigs

crisis.d: An unstable situation, in political, social, economic or military affairs, especially one involving an impending abrupt change.
crisis.e: The government took swift action to handle the water crisis.

criterion.d: A standard or test by which individual things or people may be compared and judged.
criterion.e: Criterion of choice, of decision, of selection

critic.d: A point made to criticize something.
critic.e: Bob liked most of my presentation, but offered three minor critiques.

critical.d: Inclined to find fault or criticize
critical.e: A good teacher is fair but critical.

criticism.d: The act of criticising; a critical judgment passed or expressed
criticism.e: The politician received a lot of public criticism for his controversial stance on the issue.

criticize.e: They criticized him for endangering people's lives.

crop.d: A plant, especially a cereal, grown to be harvested as food, livestock fodder or fuel or for any other economic purpose.
crop.e: the farmer had lots of crops to sell at the market

crucial.d: Essential or decisive for determining the outcome or future of something; extremely important.
crucial.e: A secure supply of crude oil is crucial for any modern nation, let alone a superpower.

cure.d: To bring (a disease or its bad effects) to an end.

curve.d: To grade on a curve (bell curve of a normal distribution).
curve.e: The teacher will curve the test.

debate.d: To participate in a debate; to dispute, argue, especially in a public arena.
debate.e: "Debate me, coward!" snarled the completely normal intellectual.

debt.d: The state or condition of owing something to another.

decent.d: Sufficiently clothed or dressed to be seen.
decent.e: Are you decent? May I come in?

declare.d: To announce one’s support, choice, opinion, etc.

decline.d: To move downwards, to fall, to drop.
decline.e: The dollar has declined rapidly since 2001.

decoration.d: The use of exotic sugars as decoys to distract the immune system of a host
decoration.e: The Diwali decorations made the house look festive.

decrease.d: An amount by which a quantity is decreased.
decrease.e: One research team has recorded Baishui’s decrease at about 27 meters per year over the last 10 years.

deeply.d: At depth, in a deep way.
deeply.e: She was deeply moved by the documentary about farmers.

defeat.d: To overcome in battle or contest.

defence.d: Government policy or (infra)structure related to the military.

defend.d: To focus one's energies and talents on preventing opponents from scoring, as opposed to focusing on scoring.
defend.e: A lawyer's duty is to defend their client in court.

delay.d: A period of time before an event occurs; the act of delaying; procrastination; lingering inactivity.
delay.e: the delay before the echo of a sound

deliberately.d: Taking one's time, slowly and carefully.
deliberately.e: After being called upon, he strode deliberately up to the blackboard.

delight.d: To give delight to; to affect with great pleasure; to please highly.
delight.e: A beautiful landscape delights the eye.

delivery.e: The delivery was completed by four.

demand.d: To claim a right to something.
demand.e: The bank is demanding the mortgage payment.

demonstrate.e: Can you demonstrate the new tools for us?

deny.d: To assert that something is not true.
deny.e: Everyone knows he committed the crime, but he still denies it.

depressing.e: The television news was depressing, so rather than start crying I turned it off.

depth.d: The intensity, complexity, strength, seriousness or importance of an emotion, situation, etc.
depth.e: The depth of her misery was apparent to everyone.

deserve.d: To be entitled to, as a result of past actions; to be worthy to have.
deserve.e: After playing so well, the team really deserved their win.

desire.d: A strong feeling of wanting something or wishing for something to happen.
desire.e: She had a strong desire to travel the world.

desperate.e: I hadn't eaten in two days and was desperate for food.

detailed.d: Characterized by attention to detail and thoroughness of treatment.

detect.d: To discover or find by careful search, examination, or probing
detect.e: Dogs can detect scents that humans cannot.

dig.d: To get by digging; to take from the ground; often with up.

disc.d: A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.
disc.e: A coin is a disc of metal.

dismiss.d: To send or put away, to discard with disregard, contempt or disdain. (sometimes followed by as).
dismiss.e: She dismissed him with a wave of the hand.

display.d: A piece of work to be presented visually.
display.e: Pupils are expected to produce a wall display about a country of their choice.

distribute.d: To divide into portions and dispense.
distribute.e: He distributed the bread amongst his followers.

distribution.d: The total number of something sold or delivered to the clients.
distribution.e: The distribution of my little rock magazine is about 3,000.

district.d: An administrative division of an area.

division.d: A calculation that involves this process.
division.e: I've got ten divisions to do for my homework.

domestic.d: Of or relating to the home.

dominate.d: To govern, rule or control by superior authority or power

downwards.d: Towards something which is lower in order, smaller, inferior, etc.
downwards.e: After the rise in oil prices, the economy headed downwards.

dozen.d: An old English measure of ore containing 12 hundredweight.
dozen.e: Can I have a dozen eggs, please?

draft.d: To conscript a person, force a person to serve in some capacity, especially in the military.
draft.e: He was drafted during the Vietnam War.

drag.d: To pull along a surface or through a medium, sometimes with difficulty.
drag.e: Let's drag this load of wood over to the shed.

dramatic.d: Of or relating to the drama.
dramatic.e: The sunset over the ocean was a dramatic sight.

edit.d: To be the editor of a publication.

edition.d: The whole number of copies of a work printed and published at one time.
edition.e: The first edition was soon sold.

efficient.d: Making good, thorough, or careful use of resources; not consuming extra. Especially, making good use of time or energy
efficient.e: An efficient process would automate all the routine work.

elderly.d: Of an object, being old-fashioned or frail due to aging.
elderly.e: The elderly couple walked hand in hand in the park.

elect.d: To choose or make a decision (to do something)

elsewhere.d: In or at some other place.

emerge.d: To come out of a situation, object or a liquid.
emerge.e: He emerged unscathed from the accident.

emotional.d: Of or relating to the emotions.

emphasis.d: Special weight or forcefulness given to something considered important.
emphasis.e: He paused for emphasis before saying who had won.

emphasize.d: To stress, give emphasis or extra weight to (something).
emphasize.e: His two-fingered gesture emphasized what he had told his boss to do with his job.

enable.d: To imply or tacitly confer excuse for an action or a behavior.
enable.e: His parents enabled him to go on buying drugs.

encounter.d: To engage in conflict, as with an enemy.

engage.d: To enter into (an activity), to participate (construed with in).
engage.e: The teacher used games to engage the students in learning.

enhance.d: To be raised up; to grow larger.
enhance.e: A debt enhances rapidly by compound interest.

enquiry.d: Search for truth, information or knowledge.

ensure.d: To make sure or certain of something (usually some future event or condition).
ensure.e: I use an alarm clock to ensure that I get up on time.

enthusiasm.d: Something in which one is keenly interested.
enthusiasm.e: She showed great enthusiasm for the science project.

entire.d: Having a smooth margin without any indentation.
entire.e: We had the entire building to ourselves for the evening.

entirely.d: To the full or entire extent.
entirely.e: It is entirely up to you where we go, as I’ll be happy with anywhere.

establish.d: To prove and cause to be accepted as true; to establish a fact; to demonstrate.
establish.e: The school was established in nineteen fifty.

estate.d: The collective property and liabilities of someone, especially a deceased person.

estimate.d: To calculate roughly, often from imperfect data.
estimate.e: The contractor gave an estimate for the house repairs.

ethical.d: Of or relating to the study of ethics.
ethical.e: The philosopher Kant is particularly known for his ethical writings.

evaluate.d: To compute or determine the value of (an expression).

evil.d: Having harmful qualities; not good; worthless or deleterious.
evil.e: an evil beast; an evil plant; an evil crop

examination.d: Particularly, an inspection by a medical professional to establish the extent and nature of any sickness or injury.
examination.e: The board examination results will be announced next week.

excuse.d: To provide an excuse for; to explain, with the aim of alleviating guilt or negative judgement.
excuse.e: You know he shouldn't have done it, so don't try to excuse his behavior!

executive.d: Of, pertaining to, or having responsibility for the day-to-day running of an organisation, business, country, etc.

existence.d: The state of being, existing, or occurring; beinghood.
existence.e: In order to destroy evil, we must first acknowledge its existence.

expectation.d: The act or state of expecting or looking forward to an event as about to happen.

expense.d: A spending or consuming, often a disbursement of funds.
expense.e: Buying the car was a big expense, but will be worth it in the long run.

exploration.d: The process of penetrating, or ranging over for purposes of (especially geographical) discovery.
exploration.e: The exploration of unknown areas was often the precursor to colonization.

expose.d: To reveal, uncover, make visible, bring to light, introduce to.
expose.e: The journalist exposed corruption in the local government.

extend.d: Of a class: to be an extension or subtype of, or to be based on, a prototype or a more abstract class.
extend.e: The classes Person and Dog extend the class Animal.

extent.d: The space, area, volume, etc., to which something extends.
extent.e: The extent of his knowledge of the language is a few scattered words.

external.d: Outside of something; on the exterior.
external.e: This building has some external pipework.

extraordinary.e: an extraordinary professor in a German university

facility.d: Dexterity of speech or action; skill, talent.
facility.e: The facility she shows in playing the violin is unrivalled.

failure.d: An object, person or endeavour in a state of failure or incapable of success.
failure.e: Failure is a stepping stone to success.

faith.d: A trust or confidence in the intentions or abilities of a person, object, or ideal from prior empirical evidence.
faith.e: I have faith in the goodness of my fellow man.

fault.d: A defect; something that detracts from perfection.
fault.e: No! This is my fault, not yours.

feather.d: A branching, hair-like structure that grows on the bodies of birds, used for flight, swimming, protection and display.

fee.d: A prize or reward. Only used in the set phrase "A finder's fee" in Modern English.
fee.e: The admission fee for the museum is fifty rupees.

feedback.d: Critical assessment of a process or activity or of their results.
feedback.e: After you hand in your essays, I will give both grades and feedback.

figure.d: To write over or under the bass, as figures or other characters, in order to indicate the accompanying chords.
figure.e: He cut a sorry figure standing there in the rain.

finance.d: To manage financially; be financier for; provide or obtain funding for a transaction or undertaking.
finance.e: He financed his home purchase through a local credit union.

finding.d: A result of research or an investigation.

firm.d: A business partnership; the name under which it trades.

flame.d: The visible part of fire; a stream of burning vapour or gas, emitting light and heat.
flame.e: The cello has a two-piece back with a beautiful narrow flame.

flash.d: To cause to shine briefly or intermittently.
flash.e: He flashed the light at the water, trying to see what made the noise.

flexible.d: Capable or being adapted or molded in some way.
flexible.e: This was a principle more flexible to their purpose. -Rogers.

folding.d: Designed to fold; as a folding bed, a folding bicycle, a folding chair, etc.

forgive.d: To pardon; to waive any negative feeling or desire for punishment, retribution, or compensation.
forgive.e: Forgive others, not because they deserve forgiveness, but because you deserve peace.

former.d: First of aforementioned two items. Used with the, often without a noun.
former.e: I drive two vehicles, a Chevy Camaro and an AMC Gremlin. I won the former on a game show.

fortune.e: She read my fortune. Apparently I will have a good love life this week, but I will have a bad week for money.

found.d: To gain, as the object of desire or effort.
found.e: to find leisure; to find means

freedom.d: The state of being free, of not being imprisoned or enslaved.
freedom.e: Having recently been released from prison, he didn't know what to do with his newfound freedom.

frequency.d: The property of occurring often rather than infrequently.
frequency.e: The FAQ addresses questions that come up with some frequency.

fully.d: In a full manner; without lack or defect.

fund.d: A money-management operation, such as a mutual fund.
fund.e: Several major funds were declared insolvent recently.

fundamental.d: Essential, as an element, principle, or law; important; original; elementary.
fundamental.e: A need for belonging seems fundamental to humans.

funding.d: The action of the verb fund.
funding.e: The council is providing funding to the church to repair the roof.

furthermore.d: In addition; besides; what's more; used to denote additional information.
furthermore.e: He skillfully took charge of the event, and furthermore, he stayed late after it to clean up.

gain.d: To have or receive advantage or profit; to acquire gain; to grow rich; to advance in interest, health, or happiness; to make progress.

gang.d: A number going in company; a number of friends or persons associated for a particular purpose.
gang.e: The gang from our office is going out for drinks Friday night.

generate.d: To produce as a result of a chemical or physical process.
generate.e: Adding concentrated sulphuric acid to water generates heat.

genre.d: A kind; a stylistic category or sort, especially of literature or other artworks.
genre.e: The computer game Half-Life redefined the first-person shooter genre.

govern.d: To exercise a deciding or determining influence on.
govern.e: Chance governs the outcome of many card games.

grab.d: To make a sudden grasping or clutching motion (at something).
grab.e: The suspect suddenly broke free and grabbed at the policeman's gun.

gradually.d: In a gradual manner; making slow progress; slowly.
gradually.e: The weather gradually became cooler as winter approached.

grand.d: Having higher rank or more dignity, size, or importance than other persons or things of the same name.
grand.e: The Grand Viziers of the Ottoman Empire.

grant.d: The thing or property granted; a gift; a boon.
grant.e: I got a grant from the government to study archeology in Egypt.

guarantee.d: Anything that assures a certain outcome.
guarantee.e: Can you give me a guarantee that he will be fit for the match?

handle.d: To behave in a particular way when handled (managed, controlled, directed).

harm.d: To cause injury to another; to hurt; to cause damage to something.
harm.e: Although not physically injured in the car accident, she received some psychological harm.

harmful.d: Of a kind likely to be damaging; injurious
harmful.e: Wear a hat to protect your skin from harmful sunlight.

hearing.d: A proceeding at which discussions are heard.
hearing.e: There will be a public hearing to discuss the new traffic light.

heel.d: The rear part of the foot, where it joins the leg.
heel.e: He drove the heel of his hand into the man's nose.

hesitate.d: To stop or pause respecting decision or action; to be in suspense or uncertainty as to a determination.
hesitate.e: He hesitated whether to accept the offer or not; men often hesitate in forming a judgment.

hollow.d: Without substance; having no real or significant worth; meaningless.

holy.d: Dedicated to a religious purpose or a god.
holy.e: I'm planning to visit the holy city of Jerusalem this Christmas.

honour.d: To confer (bestow) an honour or privilege upon (someone)
honour.e: Ten members of the profession were honored at the ceremony.

household.d: Collectively, all the persons who live in a given house; a family including attendants, servants etc.; a domestic or family establishment.

housing.d: The activity of enclosing something or providing a residence for someone.

humorous.d: Full of humor or arousing laughter; funny.
humorous.e: The writer is known for his humorous short stories.

humour.d: The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.
humour.e: She has a great sense of humour, and I always laugh a lot whenever we get together.

hunting.d: The act of finding and killing a wild animal, either for sport or with the intention of using its parts to make food, clothes, etc.

illustrate.d: To clarify something by giving, or serving as, an example or a comparison.
illustrate.e: We illustrate our definitions by including quotations or simple examples.

illustration.d: The act of illustrating; the act of making clear and distinct; education; also, the state of being illustrated, or of being made clear and distinct.
illustration.e: The children's book had colourful illustrations on every page.

imagination.d: The image-making power of the mind; the act of mentally creating or reproducing an object not previously perceived; the ability to create such images.
imagination.e: Imagination is one of the most advanced human faculties.

imply.e: The proposition that "all dogs are mammals" implies that my dog is a mammal

impose.d: To establish or apply by authority.

impress.d: To make an impression, to be impressive.
impress.e: Henderson impressed in his first game as captain.

inch.d: A unit of length equal to one twelfth of a foot, or exactly 2.54 centimetres.

incident.d: A (relatively minor) event that is incidental to, or related to others.
incident.e: It was an incident that he hoped to forget.

income.d: A newcomer or arrival; an incomer.
income.e: Her monthly income allows her to save for the future.

increasingly.e: Online shopping is becoming increasingly popular in India.

industrial.d: Belonging or pertaining to the genre of industrial music.
industrial.e: a track with clashing industrial beats

infection.d: The act or process of infecting.
infection.e: Keep the wound clean to prevent infection.

inform.e: His sense of religion informs everything he writes.

initial.d: Chronologically first, early; of or pertaining to the beginning, cause or origin.
initial.e: Our initial admiration for their efficiency gave way to disgust about their methods.

initially.e: Initially, I found city life incredibly difficult, but now I'm used to it.

inner.d: Being or occurring (farther) inside, situated farther in, located (situated) or happening on the inside of something, situated within or farther within contained within something.
inner.e: inner door;  inner room;  inner sanctum;  inner surface

insight.d: Power of acute observation and deduction
insight.e: The book offers valuable insights into Indian history.

insist.d: To stand (on); to rest (upon); to lean (upon).
insist.e: I insist that my secretary dresses nicely.

inspire.d: To infuse into; to affect, as with a superior or supernatural influence; to fill with what animates, enlivens or exalts; to communicate inspiration to.
inspire.e: Elders should inspire children with sentiments of virtue.

install.d: To connect, set up or prepare something for use.
install.e: I haven't installed the new operating system yet because of all the bugs.

instance.d: A token; a sign; a symptom or indication.
instance.e: It sends some precious instance of itself/ After the thing it loves. Hamlet IV. v. ca. 1602

institute.d: An organization founded to promote a cause
institute.e: I work in a medical research institute.

institution.e: The institution of higher speed limits was a popular move but increased the severity of crashes.

insurance.e: After five years in banking, I switched to insurance.

intended.d: Made tense; stretched out; extended; forcible; violent.

internal.d: Concerned with the domestic affairs of a nation, state or other political community.
internal.e: The nation suffered from internal conflicts.

interpret.e: He interpreted at the meeting between the Chinese and French associates.

interrupt.d: To disturb or halt (an ongoing process or action, or the person performing it) by interfering suddenly.
interrupt.e: A maverick politician repeatedly interrupted the debate by shouting.

investigation.d: The act of investigating; the process of inquiring into or following up; research, especially patient or thorough inquiry or examination
investigation.e: Despite thorough investigation, the perpetrator of the attacks remains unknown.

investment.d: The act of investing, or state of being invested.
investment.e: Giving your children a good education is a wise long-term investment.

joy.d: A feeling of extreme happiness or cheerfulness, especially related to the acquisition or expectation of something good.
joy.e: They will be a source of strength and joy in your life.

judgement.d: The power or faculty of performing such operations; especially, when unqualified, the faculty of judging or deciding rightly, justly, or wisely

junior.d: Belonging to a younger person, or an earlier time of life.

justice.d: The state or characteristic of being just or fair.

justify.d: To provide an acceptable explanation for.
justify.e: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

labour.d: Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.

landscape.d: A portion of land or territory which the eye can comprehend in a single view, including all the objects it contains.
landscape.e: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years

largely.d: In a widespread or large manner.
largely.e: They were largely successful in their efforts.

launch.d: To cause (a rocket, balloon, etc., or the payload thereof) to begin its flight upward from the ground.
launch.e: NASA launched several unmanned rockets before launching any of the Mercury astronauts.

leadership.d: The capacity of someone to lead others.
leadership.e: Good leadership is essential for a successful team.

league.d: A group or association of cooperating members.

lean.d: To incline, deviate, or bend, from a vertical position; to be in a position thus inclining or deviating.
lean.e: She leaned out of the window.

licence.d: A legal document giving official permission to do something; a permit.
licence.e: In order to enter the building, I need to show my license.

limited.d: With certain (often specified) limits placed upon it.
limited.e: I have a limited understanding of quantum physics.

lively.e: The market was lively with vendors selling fresh produce.

load.d: To put a load on or in (a means of conveyance or a place of storage).
load.e: The dock workers refused to load the ship.

loan.d: The contract and array of legal or ethical obligations surrounding a loan.
loan.e: He made a payment on his loan.

logical.d: Non-physical or conceptual yet underpinned by something physical or actual.
logical.e: Logical memory appears contiguous to an application program, but may well be stored on several physical devices, including in RAM and on hard-disks, as determined by the operating system.

long-term.d: Becoming evident after a relatively long time period.
long-term.e: Regular exercise has many long-term health benefits.

loose.d: Not fixed in place tightly or firmly.
loose.e: This wheelbarrow has a loose wheel.

lord.d: The master of the servants of a household; the master of a feudal manor

lower.d: To let descend by its own weight, as something suspended; to let down
lower.e: lower a bucket into a well

lung.d: A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.
lung.e: He hasn't the lungs to play long rallies like he use to.

maintain.d: To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
maintain.e: It is important to maintain a clean and tidy workspace.

majority.d: More than half (50%) of some group.
majority.e: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.

mass.e: There is evidence of mass extinctions in the distant past.

massive.d: Very large in size or extent
massive.e: Compared to its counterparts from World War II, the Abrams main battle tank is truly massive.

master.d: To learn to a high degree of proficiency.
master.e: It took her years to master the art of needlecraft.

matching.d: The same as another; sharing the same design.
matching.e: We bought a new sofa and matching armchairs.

maximum.d: The greatest value of a set or other mathematical structure, especially the global maximum or a local maximum of a function
maximum.e: Use the proper dose for the maximum effect.

means.d: (now chiefly in the plural) A method or course of action used to achieve some result.
means.e: He was living beyond his means.

measurement.d: Magnitude (or extent or amount) determined by an act of measuring.
measurement.e: Accurate measurement is crucial in scientific experiments.

melt.d: To change (or to be changed) from a solid state to a liquid state, usually by a gradual heat.
melt.e: I melted butter to make a cake.

military.d: Characteristic of members of the armed forces.
military.e: She was dishonorably discharged from all military duties.

mineral.d: Any naturally occurring inorganic material that has a (more or less) definite chemical composition and characteristic physical properties.

minimum.d: A period of minimum brightness or energy intensity (of a star).
minimum.e: The Maunder minimum of the Sun reportedly corresponded to a period of great cold on Earth.

minister.d: A politician who heads a ministry (national or regional government department for public service).
minister.e: He was newly appointed to be Minister of the Interior.

minor.e: The physical appearance of a candidate is a minor factor in recruitment.

minority.d: The state of being a minor; youth, the period of a person's life prior to reaching adulthood.
minority.e: Most people agreed, but a sizable minority were upset by the decision.

mission.d: A settlement or building serving as a base for missionary work.
mission.e: Many cities across the Americas grew from Spanish missions.

mixed.d: Having two or more separate aspects.
mixed.e: I get a very mixed feeling from this puzzling painting.

modify.e: The engineer modified the design to improve safety.

monitor.d: A device similar to a television set used as to give a graphical display of the output from a computer.
monitor.e: The information flashed up on the monitor.

moral.d: Of or relating to principles of right and wrong in behaviour, especially for teaching right behaviour.

motor.d: A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.

mount.d: To attach (an object) to a support, backing, framework etc.
mount.e: to mount a mailbox on a post

multiple.e: My Swiss Army knife has multiple blades.

multiply.d: To be a factor in a multiplication with (another factor).
multiply.e: He had been multiplying, but it occurred to him he needed to resolve the exponents, first.
