mysterious.e: He received a mysterious phone call this morning.

neat.d: Conditions with a liquid reagent or gas performed with no standard solvent or cosolvent.
neat.e: The Arbuzov reaction is performed by adding the bromide to the phosphite, neat.  The molecular beam was neat acetylene.

negative.d: Refusal or withholding of assents; prohibition, veto

nerve.d: A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
nerve.e: The nerves can be seen through the skin.

nevertheless.d: In spite of what preceded; yet.
nevertheless.e: The task was difficult; nevertheless, she completed it on time.

nightmare.d: A very bad or frightening dream.
nightmare.e: I had a nightmare that I tried to run but could neither move nor breathe.

notion.d: An invention; an ingenious device; a knickknack.

numerous.e: There are numerous definitions of the word 'man'.

obey.d: To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
obey.e: Students must obey the school rules.

objective.d: Of or relating to a material object, actual existence or reality.
objective.e: The objective of the meeting was to finalise the project plan.

obligation.d: A legal agreement stipulating a specified action or forbearance by a party to the agreement; the document containing such agreement.
obligation.e: X shall be entitled to subcontract its obligation to provide the Support Services. <>

observation.d: The act of observing, and the fact of being observed (see observance)
observation.e: His observation about the changing weather patterns was correct.

observe.d: To notice or view, especially carefully or with attention to detail.
observe.e: From this vantage point we can observe the behavior of the animals in their natural habitat.

obtain.d: To secure (that) a specific objective or state of affairs be reached.
obtain.e: She obtained a scholarship for her postgraduate studies.

occasionally.d: On the occasion of something else happening; incidentally, by the way.
occasionally.e: He occasionally visits his hometown in Bihar.

offence.d: The state of being offended or displeased; anger; displeasure.
offence.e: Littering in public places is a punishable offence.

offend.d: To feel or become offended; to take insult.
offend.e: Don't worry. I don't offend easily.

offensive.d: Relating to an offense or attack, as opposed to defensive.
offensive.e: The army's offensive capabilities. An offensive weapon.

opening.d: An act or instance of making or becoming open.
opening.e: He remembered fondly the Christmas morning opening of presents.

operate.d: To put into, or to continue in, operation or activity; to work.

opponent.d: One who opposes another; one who works or takes a position against someone or something; one who attempts to stop the progress of someone or something.
opponent.e: During the crackdown, many opponents of the regime were arrested.

oppose.d: To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.
oppose.e: There is still time to oppose this plan.

opposition.d: The action of opposing or of being in conflict.
opposition.e: The opposition raised concerns about the new tax policy.

organ.d: A larger part of an organism, composed of tissues that perform similar functions.

origin.d: The source of a river, information, goods, etc.
origin.e: The Ganges has its origin in the Himalayas.

otherwise.e: I’m not well today, otherwise I would have helped.  You have to open your umbrella, otherwise you'll get wet.

outcome.d: The anticipated or desired results or evidence of a learning experience (often used in the phrase learning outcomes).
outcome.e: The outcomes of this course are outlined in your syllabus.

overall.e: Overall, there is not enough evidence to form a clear conclusion.

owe.d: To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
owe.e: I owe my success to my parents and teachers.

pace.d: To walk back and forth in a small distance.
pace.e: She walked at a brisk pace to reach the station on time.

panel.d: A type of GUI widget, such as a control panel.

parliament.d: Parliament cake, a type of gingerbread.
parliament.e: Following the general election, Jane Doe took her oath of office as a member of the nation's fifth parliament.

participant.e: All participants must adhere to the rules of the competition.

partly.d: In part, or to some degree, but not completely.
partly.e: they partly finished the house so they could move in before winter;  the car was partly visible in the garage

passage.d: A paragraph or section of text or music with particular meaning.
passage.e: She struggled to play the difficult passages.

permanent.d: Lasting for an indefinitely long time.
permanent.e: The countries are now locked in a permanent state of conflict.

permit.d: An artifact or document rendering something allowed or legal.
permit.e: A construction permit can be obtained from the town offices.

phenomenon.d: A kind or type of phenomenon (sense 1 or 2)
phenomenon.e: A volcanic eruption is an impressive phenomenon.

philosophy.d: An academic discipline that seeks truth through reasoning rather than empiricism.
philosophy.e: Philosophy is often divided into five major branches: logic, metaphysics, epistemology, ethics and aesthetics.

pile.d: To cover with heaps; or in great abundance; to fill or overfill; to load.
pile.e: We piled the camel with our loads.

pitch.d: A sticky, gummy substance secreted by trees; sap.
pitch.e: It is hard to get this pitch off my hand.

plain.d: Not decorated or elaborate; simple in character or appearance.
plain.e: She prefers plain clothes without patterns.

pointed.d: In animals, having a coat pattern with points, that is, darkening of the extremities.
pointed.e: The Siamese is a pointed breed of cat.

popularity.d: The quality or state of being popular; especially, the state of being esteemed by, or of being in favor with, the people at large
popularity.e: Politicians are rarely known for their popularity.

pose.d: To place in an attitude or fixed position, for the sake of effect.
pose.e: To pose a model for a picture.

possess.d: To take control of someone's body or mind, especially in a supernatural manner.
possess.e: They thought he was possessed by evil spirits.

potential.d: A potential field is an irrotational (static) field.
potential.e: Even from a young age it was clear that she had the potential to become a great musician.

praise.d: To give praise to; to commend, glorify, or worship.
praise.e: Be sure to praise Bobby for his excellent work at school this week.

pregnant.d: Carrying developing offspring within the body.
pregnant.e: I went to the doctor and, guess what, I'm pregnant!

preparation.d: The act of preparing or getting ready.
preparation.e: Good preparation is the key to success in any exam.

presence.d: The fact or condition of being present, or of being within sight or call, or at hand.
presence.e: Any painter can benefit from the presence of a live model from which to draw.

preserve.d: To protect; to keep from harm or injury.
preserve.e: Every people have the right to preserve its identity and culture.

prime.d: First in importance, degree, or rank.
prime.e: Our prime concern here is to keep the community safe.

principle.d: A fundamental assumption or guiding belief.
principle.e: We need some sort of principles to reason from.

priority.d: A goal of a person or an organisation.
priority.e: She needs to get her priorities straight and stop playing games.

privacy.d: The state of being secluded from the presence, sight, or knowledge of others.
privacy.e: I need my privacy, so please stay out of my room.

procedure.d: The set of established forms or methods of an organized body for accomplishing a certain task or tasks.
procedure.e: Ensure that you follow procedure when accessing customers' personal information.

project.d: To make plans for; to forecast.
project.e: The CEO is projecting the completion of the acquisition by April 2007.

proof.d: An effort, process, or operation designed to establish or discover a fact or truth; an act of testing; a test; a trial.

proposal.d: Something which is proposed, or offered for consideration or acceptance
proposal.e: The committee accepted the proposal for the new community park.

propose.d: To suggest a plan, course of action, etc.
propose.e: I propose going to see a film.

prospect.d: The region which the eye overlooks at one time; view; scene; outlook.

protection.d: The process of keeping (something or someone) safe.

psychologist.d: An expert in the field of psychology.
psychologist.e: She consulted a psychologist to help manage her stress.

psychology.d: The study of the human mind.
psychology.e: She is studying psychology at the university.

publication.d: The act of publishing printed or other matter.
publication.e: The publication of the research paper received wide attention.

pupil.d: A learner under the supervision of a teacher or professor.
pupil.e: Every pupil in the school participated in the sports day.

purchase.d: To constitute the buying power for a purchase, have a trading value.
purchase.e: Many aristocratic refugees' portable treasures purchased their safe passage and comfortable exile during the revolution

pure.d: Free of flaws or imperfections; unsullied.

pursue.d: To follow, travel down (a particular way, course of action etc.).
pursue.e: Her rival pursued a quite different course.

rank.d: A row of people or things organized in a grid pattern, often soldiers [the corresponding term for the perpendicular columns in such a pattern is "file"].
rank.e: The front rank kneeled to reload while the second rank fired over their heads.

rapidly.d: With speed; in a rapid manner.
rapidly.e: She packed her case rapidly and hurried out.

raw.d: Candid in a representation of unpleasant facts, conditions, etc.
raw.e: a raw description of the American political arena

realistic.d: Expressed or represented as being accurate, practicable, or not idealistic.
realistic.e: A realistic appraisal of the situation.

reasonable.d: Not excessive or immoderate; within due limits; proper.
reasonable.e: a reasonable demand, amount, or price

recall.d: To call back, bring back or summon (someone) to a specific place, station etc.
recall.e: He was recalled to service after his retirement.

recover.d: To salvage, to extricate, to rescue (a thing or person)
recover.e: They recovered three of the explorers very much alive, then another, wracked with severe hypothermia, who was taken to hospital.

reduction.d: The amount or rate by which something is reduced, e.g. in price.

regard.d: To consider, look upon (something) in a given way etc.
regard.e: He regards honesty as a duty.

regional.d: Of, or pertaining to, a specific region or district.
regional.e: Regional languages are an important part of Indian culture.

register.d: To place one's name, or have one's name placed in a register.

regret.d: To feel sorry about (a thing that has or has not happened), afterthink: to wish that a thing had not happened, that something else had happened instead.

regulation.d: The act of regulating or the condition of being regulated.

relatively.d: Proportionally, in relation to some larger scale thing.
relatively.e: He measured his success relatively, that is, competitively.

relevant.d: Directly related, connected, or pertinent to a topic.
relevant.e: His mother provided some relevant background information concerning his medical condition.

relief.d: The removal of stress or discomfort.
relief.e: I sighed with relief when I found out that my daughter hadn't got lost, but was waiting for me at home.

rely.d: (with on or upon, formerly also with in) to trust; to have confidence in; to depend.
rely.e: We rely on the local bus service for daily commuting.

remark.d: An expression, in speech or writing, of something remarked or noticed; a mention of something

representative.d: A person chosen to act or speak on behalf of a wider group.

reputation.e: The restaurant has an excellent reputation for its South Indian food.

requirement.d: A necessity or prerequisite; something required or obligatory. Its adpositions are generally of in relation to who or what has given it, on in relation to whom or what it is given to, and for in relation to what is required.
requirement.e: There was a requirement of the government on citizens for paying taxes.

rescue.d: To save from any violence, danger or evil.
rescue.e: The well-trained team rescued everyone after the avalanche.

reserve.d: To keep in store for future or special use.
reserve.e: This cake is reserved for the guests!

resident.d: Dwelling, or having an abode, in a place for a continued length of time; residing on one's own estate.
resident.e: resident in the city or in the country

resist.d: To attempt to counter the actions or effects of.
resist.e: She could not resist buying the beautiful saree.

resolve.d: To make a firm decision to do something.
resolve.e: I resolve to finish this work before I go home.

resort.d: Recourse, refuge (something or someone turned to for safety).

retain.d: To employ by paying a retainer.
retain.e: The company works hard to retain its best employees.

reveal.d: To uncover; to show and display that which was hidden.

revolution.d: A political upheaval in a government or nation state characterized by great change.
revolution.e: The Green Revolution transformed agriculture in India.

reward.d: To give a reward to or for.
reward.e: Why are you rewarding that bad behaviour?

rhythm.d: The musical instruments which provide rhythm (mainly; not or less melody) in a musical ensemble.
rhythm.e: The Baroque term basso continuo is virtually equivalent to rhythm

rid.d: To convey, as by riding; to make or do by riding.
rid.e: How many races have you ridden this year?

root.d: The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in some plants is able to perform vegetative reproduction.
root.e: This tree's roots can go as deep as twenty metres underground.

rub.d: To rub something against (a second thing).
rub.e: I rubbed the glass with the cloth.

rubber.d: The rectangular pad on the pitcher's mound from which the pitcher must pitch.
rubber.e: Jones toes the rubber and then fires to the plate.

rural.d: Relating to the countryside or to agriculture.
rural.e: Many rural areas in India now have internet access.

rush.d: To flow or move forward rapidly or noisily.
rush.e: armies rush to battle;   waters rush down a precipice.

satellite.d: Satellite TV; reception of television broadcasts via services that utilize man-made satellite technology.
satellite.e: Do you have satellite at your house?

satisfied.e: I'm satisfied with your progress in your homework, so you can watch television now.

satisfy.d: To pay to the extent of what is claimed or due.

saving.d: A reduction in cost or expenditure.
saving.e: The shift of the supplier gave us a saving of 10 percent.

scale.d: A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.
scale.e: Please rate your experience on a scale from 1 to 10.

scheme.d: A systematic plan of future action.

scream.e: He almost hit a pole, the way he came screaming down the hill.

secure.d: To get possession of; to make oneself secure of; to acquire certainly.

seek.d: To try to find; to look for; to search for.

select.d: To choose one or more elements of a set, especially a set of options.
select.e: He looked over the menu, and selected the roast beef.

selection.d: The process or act of selecting.
selection.e: The large number of good candidates made selection difficult.

self.d: One individual's personality, character, demeanor, or disposition.
self.e: one's true self; one's better self; one's former self

senior.d: Higher in rank, dignity, or office.

sensitive.d: Meant to be concealed or kept secret.

sequence.d: A set of things next to each other in a set order; a series
sequence.e: Complete the listed tasks in sequence.

session.d: An informal gathering of musicians to play music, especially improvised jazz or a similar genre.
session.e: "Are we having a recording session?" / "Yes. We've even got some session musicians to provide some brass."

settle.d: To fix one's residence in a place; to establish a dwelling place, home, or colony. (Compare settle down.)
settle.e: the Saxons who settled in Britain

shade.d: Darkness where light, particularly sunlight, is blocked.
shade.e: The old oak tree gave shade in the heat of the day.

shadow.d: That which looms as though a shadow.
shadow.e: I don't have a shadow of doubt in my mind that my plan will succeed. The shadow of fear of my being outed always affects how I live my life. I lived in her shadow my whole life.

shallow.d: Having little depth; significantly less deep than wide.
shallow.e: Saute the onions in a shallow pan.

shame.d: Uncomfortable or painful feeling due to recognition or consciousness of one's own impropriety or dishonor or something being exposed that should have been kept private.
shame.e: The teenager couldn’t bear the shame of introducing his parents.

shelter.d: A refuge, haven or other cover or protection from something.
shelter.e: During the rainstorm, we sheltered under a tree.

shock.d: To cause to be emotionally shocked, to cause (someone) to feel surprised and upset.

shocked.d: Surprised, startled, confused, or taken aback.

shooting.d: An instance of shooting with a gun or other weapon.
shooting.e: Police are hunting the people who carried out the shootings last week.

shot.d: The heavy iron ball used for the shot put.
shot.e: The shot flew twenty metres, and nearly landed on the judge's foot.

significant.d: Having a noticeable or major effect; notable.
significant.e: That was a significant step in the right direction.

significantly.d: In a significant manner or to a significant extent.
significantly.e: Her English has improved significantly since she started practising.

silence.e: When the motor stopped, the silence was almost deafening.

silk.d: A fine fiber excreted by the silkworm or other arthropod (such as a spider).
silk.e: The thread made of silk was barely visible.

sincere.d: Genuine; meaning what one says or does; heartfelt.
sincere.e: I believe he is sincere in his offer to help.

slave.d: A person who is held in servitude as the property of another person, and whose labor (and often also whose body and life) is subject to the owner's volition and control.
slave.e: a slave to passion, to strong drink, or to ambition

slide.d: An item of play equipment that children can climb up and then slide down again.
slide.e: The long, red slide was great fun for the kids.

slip.d: To cause to slip or slide off, or out of place.
slip.e: A horse slips his bridle; a dog slips his collar.

slope.d: To hold a rifle at a slope with forearm perpendicular to the body in front holding the butt, the rifle resting on the shoulder.
slope.e: The order was given to "slope arms".

solar.d: Of or pertaining to the sun; proceeding from the sun

soul.d: The spirit or essence of a person usually thought to consist of one's thoughts and personality. Often believed to live on after the person's death.
soul.e: Fifty souls were lost when the ship sank.

specialist.d: An organism that is specialized for a particular environment.
specialist.e: The tree Lepidothamnus laxifolius is a high alpine specialist found in high-altitude bog communities and in scrub.

spiritual.d: Of or pertaining to the spirit or the soul.
spiritual.e: Respect towards ancestors is an essential part of Thai spiritual practice.

split.d: Of something solid, to divide fully or partly along a more or less straight line.

sponsor.e: He was my sponsor when I applied to join the club.

stable.d: Of software: established to be relatively free of bugs, as opposed to a beta version.
stable.e: You should download the 1.9 version of that video editing software: it is the latest stable version. The newer beta version has some bugs.

stare.d: To influence in some way by looking fixedly.
stare.e: to stare a timid person into submission

status.d: A person’s condition, position or standing relative to that of others.
status.e: Superstition is highly correlated with economic status.

steady.d: Smooth and not bumpy or with obstructions.

steel.d: An artificial metal produced from iron, harder and more elastic than elemental iron; used figuratively as a symbol of hardness.

steep.d: Of a near-vertical gradient; of a slope, surface, curve, etc. that proceeds upward at an angle near vertical.
steep.e: a steep hill or mountain; a steep roof; a steep ascent; a steep barometric gradient

sticky.e: Is this tape sticky enough to stay on that surface?

stiff.d: (of an object) Rigid; hard to bend; inflexible.
stiff.e: He was eventually caught, and given a stiff fine.

stream.d: A small river; a large creek; a body of moving water confined by banks.
stream.e: He poured the milk in a thin stream from the jug to the glass.

stretch.d: To stretch the truth; to exaggerate.
stretch.e: a man apt to stretch in his report of facts

strict.d: Rigidly interpreted; exactly limited; confined; restricted.
strict.e: to understand words in a strict sense

strike.d: To hit forcibly and deliberately; to refuse to work as a form of organised protest.
strike.e: They struck off along the river.

struggle.d: To strive, to labour in difficulty, to fight (for or against), to contend.
struggle.e: During the centuries, the people of Ireland struggled constantly to assert their right to govern themselves.

submit.d: To yield or give way to another.
submit.e: They will not submit to the destruction of their rights.

sum.d: A summary; the principal points or thoughts when viewed together; the amount; the substance; compendium.
sum.e: This is the sum and substance of his objections.

surgery.d: A procedure involving major incisions to remove, repair, or replace a part of a body.
surgery.e: Many times surgery is necessary to prevent cancer from spreading.

surround.d: To pass around; to travel about; to circumnavigate.

suspect.d: To imagine or suppose (something) to be true, or to exist, without proof.
suspect.e: to suspect the presence of disease

swear.d: To take an oath, to promise.
swear.e: I swear I will tell the truth.

sweep.d: To clean (a surface) by means of a stroking motion of a broom or brush.
sweep.e: to sweep a floor, the street, or a chimney

sympathy.d: Tendency towards or approval of the aims of a movement.
sympathy.e: Many people in Hollywood were blacklisted merely because they were suspected of Communist sympathies.

tale.d: An account of an asserted fact or circumstance; a rumour; a report, especially an idle or malicious story; a piece of gossip or slander; a lie.

tank.d: The amount held by a container; a tankful.
tank.e: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

temporary.d: Not permanent; existing only for a period or periods of time.
temporary.e: The road closure is temporary and will last two weeks.

therapy.d: Attempted remediation of a health problem following a diagnosis, usually synonymous with treatment.

threat.d: An expression of intent to injure or punish another.

threaten.e: The rocks threatened the ship's survival.

thus.e: If you throw the ball thus, as I’m showing you, you’ll have better luck hitting the target.

tone.e: Her rousing speech gave an upbeat tone to the rest of the evening.

tough.e: The tent, made of tough canvas, held up to many abuses.

transfer.d: To move or pass from one place, person or thing to another.
transfer.e: to transfer the laws of one country to another; to transfer suspicion

transform.d: To change greatly the appearance or form of.
transform.e: The alchemists sought to transform lead into gold.

transition.d: The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.

treat.e: Only let my family live, I treat thee.

treatment.d: The process or manner of treating someone or something.
treatment.e: He still has nightmares resulting from the treatment he received from his captors.

trend.d: An inclination in a particular direction.

trial.d: An opportunity to test something out; a test.
trial.e: They will perform the trials for the new equipment next week.

trick.d: A single element of a magician's (or any variety entertainer's) act; a magic trick.
trick.e: And for my next trick, I will pull a wombat out of a duffel bag.

tropical.d: From or similar to a hot humid climate

truly.e: That is truly all I know.

trust.d: To place confidence in; to rely on, to confide, or have faith, in.
trust.e: We cannot trust anyone who deceives us.

truth.d: True facts, genuine depiction or statements of reality.
truth.e: The truth is that our leaders knew a lot more than they were letting on.

tube.d: Anything that is hollow and cylindrical in shape.

tune.d: The state or condition of being correctly tuned.
tune.e: This piano is not in tune.

typically.d: In a typical or common manner.
typically.e: Lunch is typically served between twelve and two o'clock.

tyre.d: The ring-shaped protective covering around a wheel which is usually made of rubber or plastic composite and is either pneumatic or solid.

ugly.d: Offensive to one's sensibilities or morality.
ugly.e: He played an ugly trick on us.

ultimately.e: Ultimately, he will have to make a decision before the end of the week.

unable.d: Not able; lacking a certain ability.
unable.e: Are you unable to mind your own business or something?

uncomfortable.e: Sharing a house with them made me uncomfortable.

unconscious.e: My sudden fright was an unconscious response.

underwear.d: Clothes worn next to the skin, underneath outer clothing.
underwear.e: Pack enough underwear for the week-long trip.

unemployed.d: Having no job despite being able and willing to work.
unemployed.e: The unemployed are a growing portion of the population.

unemployment.d: The phenomenon of joblessness in an economy.
unemployment.e: Unemployment has been considered a cause of crime.

unexpected.e: The surprise visit from her old friend was unexpected.

unfair.e: It was unfair for the boss to give larger bonuses to his friends.

union.d: The act of uniting or joining two or more things into one.

unique.d: Being the only one of its kind; unequaled, unparalleled or unmatched.
unique.e: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook

universe.d: The sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
universe.e: I think that the universe was created by a life force rather than a deity.

unknown.d: (sometimes postpositive) Not known; unidentified; not well known.

unless.d: Except on a specified condition; if not.
unless.e: I’m leaving unless I get a pay rise (AmE: raise).

unlike.d: Differently from; not in a like or similar manner.

unlikely.d: Not likely; improbable; not to be reasonably expected.
unlikely.e: It's very unlikely that you'll be able to walk perfectly after being in a cast for six months.

unnecessary.e: The automatic child-frightener made clowns unnecessary.

unpleasant.e: There was an unpleasant smell from the kitchen.

update.d: To bring (a thing) up to date.
update.e: I need to update my records to take account of the most recent transaction.

upon.d: Physically above and in contact with.
upon.e: Place the book upon the table.

upper.d: At a higher level, rank or position.

upset.d: To be upset or knocked over.
upset.e: The carriage upset when the horse bolted.

upwards.d: To a higher figure or amount.
upwards.e: The balloon floated upwards into the sky.

urban.e: Urban areas face challenges such as traffic congestion and pollution.

urge.d: To present in an urgent manner; to insist upon.
urge.e: to urge an argument; to urge the necessity of a case

value.d: To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.
value.e: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.

vary.d: To change with time or a similar parameter.
vary.e: He varies his magic tricks so as to minimize the possibility that any given audience member will see the same trick twice.

vast.d: Very large or wide (literally or figuratively).

venue.d: Sport venue: a stadium or similar building in which a sporting competition is held.
venue.e: The wedding venue was decorated with marigold flowers.

via.e: They drove from New York to Los Angeles via Omaha.

victory.d: A winged figure representing victory, common in Roman official iconography. See Winged victory.
victory.e: It was a great victory on the battlefield.

violence.d: Action which causes destruction, pain, or suffering.
violence.e: We try to avoid violence in resolving conflicts.

virtual.d: Nearly, almost. (A relatively recent development in meaning)
virtual.e: The angry peasants were a virtual army as they attacked the castle.

vision.d: The sense or ability of sight.
vision.e: He tried drinking from the pool of water, but realized it was only a vision.

visual.d: Related to or affecting the vision.
visual.e: The teacher used visual aids to explain the lesson.

vital.d: Relating to, or characteristic of life.
vital.e: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

vitamin.d: Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.
vitamin.e: Oranges are a good source of vitamin C.

volume.d: The issues of a periodical over a period of one year.
volume.e: I looked at this week's copy of the magazine. It was volume 23, issue 45.

wage.d: (often in plural) An amount of money paid to a worker for a specified quantity of work, usually calculated on an hourly basis and expressed in an amount of money per hour.
wage.e: Before her promotion, her wages were 20% less.

weakness.e: In a small number of horses, muscle weakness may progress to paralysis.

wealth.d: A great amount; an abundance or plenty.
wealth.e: She brings a wealth of knowledge to the project.

wealthy.d: Abundant in quality or quantity; profuse.
wealthy.e: The wealthy businessman donated funds to build a school.

whereas.d: In contrast; whilst on the contrary; although.
whereas.e: He came first in the race, whereas his brother came last.

wherever.d: In or to whatever place; anywhere.
wherever.e: You can sit wherever you like.

whisper.d: To mention privately and confidentially, or in a whisper.
whisper.e: The soup had just a whisper of basil.

whom.d: Used instead of 'who' as the object of a verb or preposition.

widely.d: In a wide manner; across a wide area.
widely.e: Hindi is widely spoken across northern India.

wildlife.e: The wildlife sanctuary is home to hundreds of species.

willing.d: Ready to do something that is not (can't be expected as) a matter of course.
willing.e: If my boyfriend isn't willing to change his drinking habits, I will split up with him.

wire.d: Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.
wire.e: This election is going to go right to the wire

wise.d: Showing good judgement or the benefit of experience.
wise.e: "It is a profitable thing, if one is wise, to seem foolish" - Aeschylus

witness.d: One who is called upon to witness an event or action, such as a wedding or the signing of a document.
witness.e: The bridesmaid and best man at a wedding typically serve as the witnesses.

wound.d: To hurt or injure (someone) by cutting, piercing, or tearing the skin.
wound.e: The police officer wounded the suspect during the fight that ensued.

wrap.d: To enclose or coil around an object or organism, as a form of grasping.
wrap.e: A snake wraps itself around its prey.

zone.d: Each of the five regions of the earth's surface into which it was divided by climatic differences, namely the torrid zone (between the tropics), two temperate zones (between the tropics and the polar circles), and two frigid zones (within the polar circles).
zone.e: Files in the Internet zone are blocked by default, as a security measure.

absorb.d: To take in energy and convert it, as
absorb.e: Heat, light, and electricity are absorbed in the substances into which they pass.

accent.d: A higher-pitched or stronger articulation of a particular syllable of a word or phrase in order to distinguish it from the others or to emphasize it.
accent.e: In the word "careful", the accent is placed on the first syllable.

accidentally.d: In an accidental manner; by chance, unexpectedly.

accommodate.d: To provide with something desired, needed, or convenient.
accommodate.e: to accommodate a friend with a loan

accomplish.d: To execute fully; to fulfill; to complete successfully.
accomplish.e: to accomplish a design, an object, a promise

accountant.d: A reckoner, or someone who maintains financial matters for a person(s).
accountant.e: The accountant prepared the company's annual financial report.

accuracy.d: The state of being accurate; being free from mistakes, this exemption arising from carefulness; exactness; correctness
accuracy.e: The value of testimony depends on its accuracy.

accurately.d: In an accurate manner; exactly; precisely; without error or defect.
accurately.e: She accurately translated the document from Hindi to English.

acid.d: Sour, sharp, or biting to the taste; tart; having the taste of vinegar.

activate.d: To render a molecule reactive, active, or effective in performing its function.
activate.e: Activate your account by clicking the link in the email.

activist.d: One who is conspicuously active in carrying out any occupational or professional functions.
activist.e: The environmental activist organised a beach cleanup drive.

adaptation.d: A change that is made or undergone to suit a condition or environment.
adaptation.e: The play is an adaptation of a famous Hindi novel.

addiction.d: The state of being addicted; devotion; inclination.
addiction.e: Social media addiction is a growing concern among teenagers.

adequate.d: Equal to or fulfilling some requirement.

adequately.d: Sufficient to satisfy a requirement or meet a need; sufficiently; satisfactorily.
adequately.e: The school is adequately equipped with computers and projectors.

adhere.d: To stick fast or cleave, as a glutinous substance does; to become joined or united.

adjacent.e: The picture is on the adjacent page.

adjust.e: Most immigrants adjust quickly to a new community.   She waited for her eyes to adjust to the darkness.

adjustment.d: The settling or balancing of a financial account
adjustment.e: The credit card company made an adjustment to my account to waive the late fee.

administer.d: To cause to ingest (a drug), either by openly offering or through deceit.
administer.e: We administered the medicine to our dog by mixing it in his food.

administrative.d: Of or relating to administering or administration.
administrative.e: She handles all the administrative tasks in the office.

administrator.d: One who is responsible for software installation, management, information and maintenance of a computer or network
administrator.e: The school administrator managed the admission process efficiently.

admission.d: The cost or fee associated with attendance or entry.
admission.e: There is no way he has seen that show, the admission is more than he makes in a week.

adolescent.d: A person who is in adolescence; someone who has reached puberty but is not yet an adult.

adoption.d: The state of being adopted; the acceptance of a child of other parents as if he or she were one's own child.
adoption.e: A Chinese baby girl was given away for adoption.

adverse.d: Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.

advocate.e: I like trees, but I do not advocate living in them.

aesthetic.d: Beautiful or appealing to one's sense of beauty and/or art.
aesthetic.e: It works well enough, but the shabby exterior offends his aesthetic sensibilities.

affection.d: A feeling of love or strong attachment.
affection.e: I have a lot of affection for my little sister.

affordable.d: Able to be afforded; inexpensive or reasonably priced.
affordable.e: an affordable vacation in the local area

aftermath.d: That which happens after, that which follows, usually of strongly negative connotation in most contexts, implying a preceding catastrophe.
aftermath.e: In contrast to most projections of the aftermath of nuclear war, in this there is no rioting or looting.

aggression.d: The practice or habit of launching attacks.
aggression.e: Aggression should not be used to resolve conflicts.

agriculture.d: The art or science of cultivating the ground, including the harvesting of crops, and the rearing and management of livestock
agriculture.e: Agriculture is the main livelihood in rural India.

aide.d: An officer who acts as assistant to a more senior one; an aide-de-camp.
aide.e: The minister's aide arranged the official meetings.

aids.d: An aide-de-camp, so called by abbreviation.
aids.e: The incompetent general's brilliant aid often made priceless suggestions.

albeit.e: The hotel was small, albeit comfortable and clean.

align.d: To identify with or match the behaviour, thoughts, etc of another person.
align.e: Please align the text to the left margin.

alignment.d: The process of adjusting a mechanism such that its parts are aligned; the condition of having its parts so adjusted.
alignment.e: The alignment of the wheels needs to be checked.

alike.d: Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.

allegation.d: An assertion, especially an accusation, not necessarily based on facts.
allegation.e: She put forth several allegations regarding her partner in hopes of discrediting his actions.

allege.d: To make a claim as justification or proof; to make an assertion without proof.
allege.e: The agency alleged my credit history had problems.

allegedly.d: According to someone's conspicuous impressions (which could be alleged ensuingly).

alliance.e: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries

allocate.d: To distribute according to a plan, generally followed by the adposition to.
allocate.e: The bulk of K–12 education funds are allocated to school districts that in turn pay for the cost of operating schools.

allocation.d: The process or procedure for allocating things, especially money or other resources.
allocation.e: The allocation of new permits is on a first-come, first-served basis.

allowance.e: her meagre allowance of food or drink

ally.d: A closely related species, usually within the same family.

alongside.d: Together with or at the same time.
alongside.e: The nurse worked alongside the doctor.

altogether.d: On the whole; with everything considered.

aluminium.d: Aircraft or other machinery made partially or wholly of aluminium.

amateur.d: Created, done, or populated by amateurs or non-professionals.

ambassador.d: A corporate representative, often the public face of the company.
ambassador.e: As front hall porter, you are an ambassador for the hotel.

ambulance.d: An emergency vehicle designed for transporting seriously ill or injured people to a hospital.
ambulance.e: The ambulance arrived within minutes of the call.

amend.d: To be healed, to be cured, to recover (from an illness).
amend.e: The parliament voted to amend the existing law.

amendment.d: An addition to and/or alteration to the Constitution.
amendment.e: The First Amendment guarantees freedom of religion, speech, press, assembly, and petition.

amid.d: In the middle of; in the center of; surrounded by.
amid.e: She remained calm amid the chaos around her.

amusing.d: Causing laughter and providing entertainment; funny.
amusing.e: He told an amusing story about his childhood.

analogy.d: A relationship of resemblance or equivalence between two situations, people, or objects, especially when used as a basis for explanation or extrapolation.
analogy.e: The teacher used an analogy to explain the complex topic.

analyst.d: A financial analyst; a business analyst.
analyst.e: The financial analyst predicted a rise in the stock market.

ancestor.d: One from whom a person is descended, whether on the father's or mother's side, at any distance of time; a progenitor; a forefather.
ancestor.e: This fossil animal is regarded as the ancestor of the horse.

anchor.d: A tool used to moor a vessel to the bottom of a sea or river to resist movement.

angel.d: A person having the qualities attributed to angels, such as purity or selflessness.
angel.e: Thanks for making me breakfast in bed, you little angel.

animation.d: The act of animating, or giving life or spirit.
animation.e: He recited the story with great animation.

annually.d: Once every year without fail, yearly
annually.e: The festival is held annually in the month of January.

anonymous.d: Without any name acknowledged of a person responsible

anticipate.d: To take up or introduce (something) prematurely.
anticipate.e: The advocate plans to anticipate a part of her argument.

anxiety.d: An unpleasant state of mental uneasiness, nervousness, apprehension and obsession or concern about some uncertain event.
anxiety.e: She felt anxiety before the important exam.

apology.d: An expression of remorse or regret for having said or done something that harmed another: an instance of apologizing (saying that one is sorry).
apology.e: The CEO made a public apology for the scandal, and promised full cooperation with the authorities.

applicant.d: One who applies for something; one who makes a request; a petitioner.
applicant.e: Our job advertisement attracted seven applicants.

appropriately.d: In an appropriate manner; properly; suitably.
appropriately.e: Please dress appropriately for the formal dinner.

arrow.d: A projectile consisting of a shaft, a point and a tail with stabilizing fins that is shot from a bow.

artwork.d: A painting, drawing, sculpture or other piece of creative, visual art
artwork.e: The text has been reviewed, but the photographer hasn't delivered some of the artwork.

aside.d: To or on one side so as to be out of the way.
aside.e: Move aside, please, so that these people can come through.

asset.d: Something or someone of any value; any portion of one's property or effects so considered.
asset.e: These shares are a valuable asset.

assign.d: To designate or set apart something for some purpose.
assign.e: to assign a day for trial

assistance.d: Aid; help; the act or result of assisting.
assistance.e: The organisation provides financial assistance to needy students.

assumption.d: The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.
assumption.e: His assumption of secretarial duties was timely.

assure.e: He assured of his commitment to her happiness.

attachment.d: A strong bonding with or fondness for someone or something.
attachment.e: I have such an attachment towards my fiancé!

auction.d: A public event where goods or property are sold to the highest bidder.

audio.d: Focused on audible sound, as opposed to sight.
audio.e: The audio quality of the recording was excellent.

automatic.d: Capable of operating without external control or intervention.
automatic.e: The automatic clothes washer was a great labor-saving device.

automatically.e: The doors of the metro train open automatically.

awareness.d: The state or quality of being aware of something
awareness.e: The awareness of one type of idea naturally fosters an awareness of another idea

awkward.d: Lacking dexterity in the use of the hands, or of instruments.
awkward.e: John was awkward at performing the trick. He'll have to practice to improve.

badge.d: A distinctive mark, token, sign, emblem or cognizance, worn on one's clothing, as an insignia of some rank, or of the membership of an organization.
badge.e: the badge of a society; the badge of a policeman

balanced.d: Containing elements in appropriate proportion; proportionately weighted on all dimensions and therefore unlikely to tip over.
balanced.e: He believed he rarely got sick because of his balanced diet.

balloon.d: An inflatable buoyant object, often (but not necessarily) round and flexible.
balloon.e: the balloon of St. Paul's Cathedral in London

barely.e: I could barely hear what she said.

bargain.d: An agreement between parties concerning the sale of property; or a contract by which one party binds himself to transfer the right to some property for a consideration, and the other party binds himself to receive the property and pay the consideration.
bargain.e: At that price, it's not just a bargain, it's a steal.

basement.d: A floor of a building below ground level.
basement.e: The parking area is in the basement of the building.

basket.d: A lightweight container, generally round, open at the top, and tapering toward the bottom.
basket.e: A basket of fake fruit adorned the table.

bat.d: To hit with a bat or (figuratively) as if with a bat.
bat.e: He batted the ball away with a satisfying thwack.

beneficial.d: Helpful or good to something or someone.
beneficial.e: Recycling and reusing garbage can be beneficial to the environment.

beside.d: Next to; at the side of.
beside.e: A small table beside the bed

besides.d: Used to emphasize an additional point, especially an important or stronger reason; Moreover; furthermore.
besides.e: I don't feel like going out tonight. Besides, I have to work tomorrow morning anyway.

bias.d: Inclination towards something; predisposition, partiality, prejudice, preference, predilection.

bid.d: To make an offer to pay or accept a certain price.
bid.e: Have you ever bid in an auction?

biological.e: The biological diversity of the Western Ghats is remarkable.

blanket.d: A heavy, loosely woven fabric, usually large and woollen, used for warmth while sleeping or resting.
blanket.e: The baby was cold, so his mother put a blanket over him.

bold.e: Bold deeds win admiration and, sometimes, medals.

bombing.d: The action of dropping bombs from the air.

booking.d: The act or process of writing something down in a book or books, e.g. in accounting.

boost.d: To give a booster shot to.
boost.e: The controversy gave a boost to the author's sales.

bound.e: You are not legally bound to reply.

brick.d: A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.
brick.e: This wall is made of bricks.

briefly.e: Briefly, I am not happy about what happened, but no one will be losing their job.

broadcaster.d: An organisation that engages in the activity of broadcasting.
broadcaster.e: The news broadcaster reported live from the flood-affected area.

broadly.d: In a wide manner; liberally; in a loose sense.
broadly.e: The plan was broadly welcomed by the community.

bug.d: An insect of the order Hemiptera (the “true bugs”).
bug.e: These flies are a bother. I’ll get some bug spray and kill them.

cabin.d: A small dwelling characteristic of the frontier, especially when built from logs with simple tools and not constructed by professional builders, but by those who meant to live in it.
cabin.e: Abraham Lincoln was born in a log cabin.

canal.d: An artificial waterway or artificially improved river used for travel, shipping, or irrigation.

candle.d: A light source consisting of a wick embedded in a solid, flammable substance such as wax, tallow, or paraffin.

carbon.d: The chemical element (symbol C) with an atomic number of 6. It can be found in pure form for example as graphite, a black, shiny and very soft material, or diamond, a colourless, transparent, crystalline solid and the hardest known material.
carbon.e: A methane molecule is made up of a single carbon with four hydrogens.

cave.d: A large, naturally-occurring cavity formed underground or in the face of a cliff or a hillside.
cave.e: We found a cave on the mountainside where we could take shelter.

certainty.d: A fact or truth unquestionably established.
certainty.e: No one can say with certainty when the monsoon will arrive.

certificate.d: A document containing a certified statement.

championship.d: Defense or support of some cause.
championship.e: His championship of civil rights eventually bore fruit.

chase.d: To attempt to win by scoring the required number of runs in the final innings.
chase.e: Australia will be chasing 217 for victory on the final day.

cheek.d: The soft skin on each side of the face, below the eyes; the outer surface of the sides of the oral cavity.
cheek.e: You’ve got some cheek, asking me for money!

cheer.d: To applaud or encourage with cheers or shouts.
cheer.e: The crowd cheered in support of the athletes.

choir.d: Singing group; group of people who sing together; company of people who are trained to sing together.
choir.e: The church choir practices Thursday nights.

chop.d: To cut into pieces with short, vigorous cutting motions.

circuit.d: The act of moving or revolving around, or as in a circle or orbit; a revolution

civilization.e: A hermit doesn't much care for civilization.

clarify.d: To grow or become clear or transparent; to become free from feculent impurities, as wine or other liquid under clarification.
clarify.e: Leave the wine for 24 hours and it will clarify.

classify.d: To declare something a secret, especially a government secret
classify.e: They decided to classify that information.

clerk.d: One who occupationally works with records, accounts, letters, etc.; an office worker.

cliff.d: A vertical (or nearly vertical) rock face.
cliff.e: They stood at the edge of the cliff overlooking the sea.

clinic.d: A medical facility, such as a hospital, especially one for the treatment and diagnosis of outpatients.
clinic.e: She visited the clinic for a routine health check-up.

clip.d: Something which clips or grasps; a device for attaching one object to another.
clip.e: Use this clip to attach the check to your tax form.

coincidence.d: Of events, the appearance of a meaningful connection when there is none.
coincidence.e: Meeting her at the airport was a complete coincidence.

collector.d: A person who or thing that collects, or which creates or manages a collection.
collector.e: He is an avid collector of nineteenth-century postage stamps.

colony.d: Region or governmental unit created by another country and generally ruled by another country.
colony.e: Bermuda is a crown colony of Great Britain.

colourful.d: Profane, obscene, offensive (usually in the phrase colourful language).
colourful.e: The colourful rangoli design won first prize at the competition.

commander.d: A naval officer whose rank is above that of a lieutenant commander and below that of captain.
commander.e: The commander led the troops during the military exercise.

comparative.d: Using comparison as a method of study, or founded on something using it.

completion.d: The act of making a metric space complete by adding points.
completion.e: The completion of the metro line is expected by next year.

compose.d: To make something by merging parts.
compose.e: The editor composed a historical journal from many individual letters.

composer.e: A R Rahman is one of India's most celebrated music composers.

compound.d: An enclosure within which workers, prisoners, or soldiers are confined

comprehensive.d: Broadly or completely covering; including a large proportion of something.
comprehensive.e: When there are diametrically opposing views on a big issue that concerns millions of people, doing comprehensive research just makes sense.

comprise.d: To be made up of; to consist of (especially a comprehensive list of parts).
comprise.e: The parts are comprised by the whole.

compulsory.d: Having the power of compulsion; constraining.

concrete.e: While everyone else offered thoughts and prayers, she made a concrete proposal to help.

confess.d: To admit to the truth, particularly in the context of sins or crimes committed.
confess.e: I confess to spray-painting all over that mural!

confusion.d: A lack of clarity or order.
confusion.e: There was some confusion about the meeting time.

consequently.d: As a result or consequence of something.
consequently.e: He didn't wake up early. Consequently, he was late to work.

conservation.d: Lack of change in a measurable property of an isolated physical system (conservation of energy, mass, momentum, electric charge, subatomic particles, and fundamental symmetries)
conservation.e: Wildlife conservation is important for protecting endangered species.

considerable.e: I've spent a considerable amount of time on this.

considerably.d: Significantly; to a degree worth considering.

consistently.e: She consistently scored well in all her exams.

conspiracy.d: The act of two or more persons, called conspirators, working secretly to obtain some goal, usually understood with negative connotations.
conspiracy.e: The novel is about a conspiracy to steal ancient artifacts.

consult.d: To work as a consultant or contractor rather than as a full-time employee of a firm.
consult.e: You should consult a doctor if the pain continues.

consultant.d: A person or party that is consulted
consultant.e: The company hired a consultant to improve its marketing strategy.

consumption.d: The act of eating, drinking or using.
consumption.e: The consumption of snails as food is more common in France than in England.

controversial.d: Arousing controversy—a debate or discussion of opposing opinions.
controversial.e: The new policy proved to be highly controversial.

controversy.d: A debate or discussion of opposing opinions; (generally) strife.
controversy.e: The decision sparked a controversy in the media.

convenience.e: Fast food is popular because of its cost and convenience.

convention.d: A formal deliberative assembly of mandated delegates.
convention.e: The EU installed an inter-institutional Convention to draft a European constitution.

conventional.d: Pertaining to a convention, as in following generally accepted principles, methods and behaviour.
conventional.e: They wear conventional clothes, eat conventional food, and keep conventional hours.

convey.d: To move (something) from one place to another.
convey.e: Air conveys sound. Water is conveyed through the pipe.

convincing.e: Our convincing evidence was sufficient in the end to win the trial.

cope.d: To deal effectively with something, especially if difficult.
cope.e: I thought I would never be able to cope with life after the amputation, but I have learned how to be happy again.

corporation.d: The municipal governing body of a borough or city.
corporation.e: The multinational corporation has offices in Mumbai and Bengaluru.

corridor.d: A narrow hall or passage with rooms leading off it, as in a building or in a railway carriage.
corridor.e: The students walked quietly along the school corridor.

coverage.d: An amount by which something or someone is covered.
coverage.e: Before laying sod on that clay, the ground needs two inches of coverage with topsoil.

crack.e: The bat cracked with authority and the ball went for six.

craft.d: To construct, develop something (like a skilled craftsman).
craft.e: state crafting; the process of crafting global policing

creativity.d: The ability to use imagination to produce a novel idea or product that is useful to society.
creativity.e: His creativity is unsurpassed by his fellow students in the art class.

critically.d: In a critical manner; with, or in terms of, criticism.
critically.e: I looked critically at the frayed carpet of the hotel room.

cruise.e: Germany cruised to a World Cup victory over the short-handed Australians.

curious.d: Tending to ask questions, or to want to explore or investigate; inquisitive; (with a negative connotation) nosy, prying.
curious.e: Young children are naturally curious about the world and everything in it.

curriculum.d: The set of courses, coursework, and their content, offered at a school or university.
curriculum.e: The new curriculum includes computer science from class one.

cute.d: Mentally keen or discerning (See also acute)
cute.e: Cute trick, but can you do it consistently?

dairy.d: On food labelling, containing fats only from dairy sources (e.g. dairy ice cream).
dairy.e: The dairy farm produces fresh milk and cheese.

dare.d: To have enough courage (to do something).
dare.e: I wouldn't dare argue with my boss.

darkness.d: The state of being dark; lack of light.
darkness.e: The darkness of the room made it difficult to see.

database.d: A set of tables in a database(1).
database.e: The "books" database will have three tables, and the "customers" database will have two tables.

deadline.d: A time limit in the form of a date on or before which something must be completed.
deadline.e: I must make this deadline or my boss will kill me!

deadly.d: Aiming or willing to destroy; implacable; desperately hostile.

dealer.d: One who deals in goods, especially automobiles; a middleman.
dealer.e: That used car dealer gave me a great deal on my 1962 rusted-out Volkswagen bug!

deck.d: Any raised flat surface that can be walked on: a balcony; a porch; a raised patio; a flat rooftop.

defender.d: Someone who defends people or property
defender.e: The defender blocked the goal just in time.

delete.d: To remove, get rid of or erase, especially written or printed material, or data on a computer or other device.

democracy.d: Rule by the people, especially as a form of government; either directly or through elected representatives (representative democracy).
democracy.e: India is the largest democracy in the world.

democratic.d: Relating to a political party so called; usually, Democratic.
democratic.e: Mount Vernon is run by a strong democratic party organization.

demonstration.d: An event at which something will be demonstrated.
demonstration.e: I have to give a demonstration to the class tomorrow, and I'm ill-prepared.

depart.d: To deviate (from), be different (from), fail to conform.
depart.e: His latest statements seemed to depart from party policy somewhat.

dependent.e: At that point I was dependent on financial aid for my tuition.

deposit.d: To lay down; to place; to put.
deposit.e: A crocodile deposits her eggs in the sand.

depression.d: In psychotherapy and psychiatry, a state of mind producing serious, long-term lowering of enjoyment of life or inability to visualize a happy future.
depression.e: I used to suffer from depression, but now I'm mostly content with my life.

derive.d: To obtain or receive (something) from something else.
derive.e: Many English words derive from Latin and Greek.

desperately.d: In a desperate manner; without regard to danger or safety; recklessly.
desperately.e: The villagers desperately needed clean drinking water.

destruction.d: The results of a destructive event.
destruction.e: Amid the seemingly endless destruction, a single flower bloomed.

determination.d: The act of determining, or the state of being determined.
determination.e: Through sheer determination, we managed to escape from the tornado.

devote.d: To give one's time, focus one's efforts, commit oneself, etc. entirely for, on, or to a certain matter
devote.e: I devoted this afternoon to repainting my study, and nothing will get in my way.

disability.d: A mental condition causing a difficulty with an intellectual task.

disabled.d: Made incapable of use or action.

disagreement.d: A condition of not agreeing or concurring.
disagreement.e: The theory shows considerable disagreement with the data.

disappoint.d: To sadden or displease (someone) by underperforming, or by not delivering something promised or hoped for.
disappoint.e: His lack of respect disappointed her.

disappointment.d: That which causes feelings of disappointment.
disappointment.e: Our trip to California was a disappointment.

discourage.d: To extinguish the courage of; to dishearten; to depress the spirits of; to deprive of confidence; to deject.
discourage.e: Don't be discouraged by the amount of work left to do: you'll finish it in good time.

disorder.d: Absence of order; state of not being arranged in an orderly manner.
disorder.e: After playing the children left the room in disorder.

distant.e: We heard a distant rumbling but didn't pay any more attention to it.   She was surprised to find that her fiancé was a distant relative of hers.   His distant look showed that he was not listening to me.

distinct.e: Olga's voice is quite distinct because of her accent.

distinguish.d: To make oneself noticeably different or better from others through accomplishments.
distinguish.e: The soldier distinguished himself in combat and received a medal.

distract.e: The crowd was distracted by a helicopter hovering over the stadium when the only goal of the game was scored.

disturb.d: To divert, redirect, or alter by disturbing.
disturb.e: A mudslide disturbed the course of the river.

dive.d: To jump headfirst toward the ground or into another substance.

diverse.e: Idris Elba was a diverse hire for the franchise

diversity.d: The quality of being diverse or different; difference or unlikeness.
diversity.e: India is known for its incredible cultural diversity.

divorce.e: Edna and Simon divorced last year; he got the house, and she retained the business.

dominant.d: Predominant, common, prevalent, of greatest importance.
dominant.e: The dominant plants of the Carboniferous were lycopods and early conifers.

donation.d: A voluntary gift or contribution for a specific cause.
donation.e: They were collecting donations for the elderly at Christmas.

downtown.d: Of, relating to, or situated in the central business district
downtown.e: John walked every day to his downtown job.

dramatically.e: The city has changed dramatically over the past decade.

drought.d: A period of unusually low rainfall, longer and more severe than a dry spell.
drought.e: The drought affected farmers across the state.

dull.d: Lacking the ability to cut easily; not sharp.

dump.d: To release, especially in large quantities and chaotic manner.
dump.e: We dumped the coal onto the fireplace.

duration.d: An amount of time or a particular time interval.
duration.e: Rationing will last at least for the duration.

dynamic.d: Having to do with the volume of sound.
dynamic.e: The dynamic marking in bar 40 is forte.

economist.d: An expert in economics, especially one who studies economic data and extracts higher-level information or proposes theories.
economist.e: The economist analysed the impact of inflation on households.

editorial.d: Of or relating to an editor, editing or an editorial.

efficiently.e: She completed the project efficiently and ahead of schedule.

elbow.d: The joint between the upper arm and the forearm.
elbow.e: the sides of windows, where the jamb makes an elbow with the window back

electronics.d: The study and use of electrical devices that operate by controlling the flow of electrons or other electrically charged particles or by converting the flow of charged particles to or from other forms of energy.

elegant.d: Characterised by minimalism and intuitiveness while preserving exactness and precision.

elementary.e: He teaches at an elementary school in a small town.

eliminate.e: a ruthless mobster who eliminated his enemies

embrace.d: To seize (something) eagerly or with alacrity; to accept or take up with cordiality; to welcome.
embrace.e: I wholeheartedly embrace the new legislation.

emission.d: Something which is emitted or sent out; issue.

emotionally.d: In an emotional manner; displaying emotion.
emotionally.e: Physically, it was easy, but emotionally it was the hardest thing I've ever done.

empire.d: A political unit ruled by an emperor or empress.
empire.e: The Empire of Vietnam was a short-lived client state of Japan governing Vietnam between March 11 and August 23, 1945.

enjoyable.e: That was an enjoyable day; I had a lot of fun.

entrepreneur.d: A person who organizes a risky activity of any kind and acts substantially in the manner of a business entrepreneur.
entrepreneur.e: The young entrepreneur started a successful tech company.

envelope.d: A curve that bounds another curve or set of curves, as the modulation envelope of an amplitude-modulated carrier wave in electronics.
envelope.e: She put the letter in an envelope and posted it.

equip.e: The hospital was equipped with the latest medical technology.

equivalent.d: Similar or identical in value, meaning or effect; virtually equal.
equivalent.e: To burn calories, a thirty-minute jog is equivalent to a couple of hamburgers.

era.d: A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.
era.e: We are living in the era of digital communication.

erupt.d: To eject something violently (such as lava or water, as from a volcano or geyser).
erupt.e: The volcano erupted, spewing lava across a wide area.

essentially.d: In an essential manner; in essence
essentially.e: The two proposals are essentially the same.

ethic.d: A set of principles of right and wrong behaviour guiding, or representative of, a specific culture, society, group, or individual.
ethic.e: I think the golden rule is a great ethic.

ethnic.d: Of or relating to a group of people having common racial, ancestral, national, religious or cultural origins.
ethnic.e: There are many ethnic Indonesians in the Netherlands

evaluation.d: Determination of the value of a variable or expression.
evaluation.e: The performance evaluation helped employees identify areas for improvement.

evident.e: It was evident she was angry, after she slammed the door.

evolution.e: The museum exhibit traces the evolution of life on Earth.

evolve.d: To give off (gas, such as oxygen or carbon dioxide during a reaction).

exceed.e: The company's 2005 revenue exceeds that of 2004.

exception.d: That which is excepted or taken out from others; a person, thing, or case, specified as distinct, or not included.
exception.e: That rule is usually true, but there are a few exceptions.

excessive.d: Exceeding the usual bounds of something; extravagant; immoderate.
excessive.e: Putting a wide vibrato on a single 16th triplet note at 160 beats per minute seems rather excessive.

exclude.e: to exclude young animals from the womb or from eggs

exhibit.d: To display or show (something) for others to see, especially at an exhibition or contest.
exhibit.e: He wanted to exhibit his baseball cards.

exit.d: A way out of a building, room, or vehicle; to go out of or leave a place.

exotic.d: Foreign, especially in an exciting way.

expansion.d: The act or process of expanding.
expansion.e: The expansion of metals and plastics in response to heat is well understood.

expertise.d: Great skill or knowledge in a particular field or hobby.
expertise.e: The scientist has expertise in the field of nuclear fusion.

exploit.d: To forcibly deprive someone of something to which she or he has a natural right.
exploit.e: Materialistic monsters who exploit "kind" folks will not have good outcomes, no matter how much comforts were ill-gained.

exposure.d: The condition of being exposed, uncovered, or unprotected.
exposure.e: Limit your exposure to harsh chemicals.   Get as much exposure to a new language as you can.

extension.d: An optional software component that adds functionality to an application.

extensive.d: Having a combined system entropy that equals the sum of the entropies of the independent systems.
extensive.e: I have done extensive research on the subject.

extensively.e: He has read and traveled extensively.

extract.d: Any substance extracted is such a way, and characteristic of that from which it is obtained
extract.e: quinine is the most important extract of Peruvian bark.

fabric.d: Interconnected nodes that look like a textile fabric when diagrammed.
fabric.e: The Internet is a fabric of computers connected by routers.

fabulous.d: Of or relating to fable, myth or legend.
fabulous.e: The food at the restaurant was absolutely fabulous.

failed.d: Not achieving the desired outcome; unsuccessful.
failed.e: The experiment was a failed attempt.

fame.d: What is said or reported; gossip, rumour.

fantasy.d: That which comes from one's imagination.

fare.d: A going; journey; travel; voyage; course; passage.

federal.d: Pertaining to the national government level, as opposed to state, provincial, county, city, or town.
federal.e: India has a federal system of government.

fever.d: A higher than normal body temperature of a person (or, generally, a mammal), usually caused by disease.
fever.e: "I have a fever. I think I've the flu."

firefighter.d: A person who puts out fires.
firefighter.e: The firefighter bravely rescued the family from the burning building.

firework.d: A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and sometimes made to rocket high into the sky before exploding, used for entertainment or celebration.
firework.e: Commercial firework displays are commonly launched from boats or other vessels

firmly.d: (manner) In a firm or definite or strong manner.
firmly.e: She corrected him firmly but gently.

flavour.d: The quality produced by the sensation of taste or, especially, of taste and smell in combined effect.
flavour.e: The flavor of this apple pie is delicious.

fond.d: (chiefly with of) Having a liking or affection (for).

fool.d: A person with poor judgment or little intelligence.
fool.e: The village fool threw his own shoes down the well.

forbid.d: To oppose, hinder, or prevent, as if by an effectual command.
forbid.e: An impassable river forbids the approach of the army.

forecast.d: To estimate how something will be in the future.
forecast.e: to forecast a rise in prices

format.d: The layout of a publication or document.
format.e: The radio station changed the format of its evening program.

formation.d: A grouping of military units or smaller formations under a command, such as a brigade, division, wing, etc.
formation.e: The formation of clouds indicates a change in weather.

formerly.d: At some time in the past
formerly.e: The building was formerly used as a post office.

fortunate.d: Happening by good luck or favorable chance.
fortunate.e: Patrick was the unlikely match-winner as Berkeley earned a fortunate victory over Chisolm.

forum.d: An Internet message board where users can post messages regarding one or more topics of discussion.
forum.e: Trish was an admin on three forums, and had no trouble at all when it came to moderating them.

fossil.d: The mineralized remains of an animal or plant.
fossil.e: Fossils of dinosaurs have been found in parts of Gujarat.

foundation.d: The act of founding, fixing, establishing, or beginning to erect.
foundation.e: The foundation of his institute has been wrought with difficulty.

founder.d: One who founds or establishes (especially said of a company, project, organisation, state)
founder.e: the founder of Facebook, Mark Zuckerberg

fraction.d: A part of a whole, especially a comparatively small part.

fragment.d: A part broken off; a small, detached portion; an imperfect part, either physically or not
fragment.e: I heard a small fragment of the conversation.

framework.d: A support structure comprising joined parts or conglomerated particles and intervening open spaces of similar or larger size.
framework.e: These ‘three principles of connexion’ compose the framework of principles in Hume's account of the association of ideas.

fraud.d: The crime of stealing or otherwise illegally obtaining money by use of deception tactics.
fraud.e: The police arrested the man for bank fraud.

frequent.e: I am a frequent visitor to that city.

fulfil.d: To satisfy, carry out, bring to completion (an obligation, a requirement, etc.).
fulfil.e: You made a promise, son, and now you must fulfill it.

full-time.d: Involving a full amount of time spent on some activity, especially a job.
full-time.e: Bringing up a child is a full-time activity.

fundamentally.d: In a fundamental or basic sense; reaching the very core of the matter.
fundamentally.e: The two approaches are fundamentally different.

furious.e: a furious animal; parent furious at their child's behaviour

gaming.d: The playing of a game or games, including but not limited to video games and games of chance.

gender.d: The state of being male or female, often with reference to social and cultural differences.
gender.e: Gender equality is an important issue.

gene.d: A segment of DNA or RNA from a cell's or an organism's genome, that may take several forms and thus parameterizes a phenomenon, in general the structure of a protein; locus.
gene.e: A change in a gene is reflected in the protein or RNA molecule that it codes for.

genetic.d: Based on shared membership in a linguistic family.
genetic.e: Chinese has borrowed several words from English, but it does not have a genetic relationship to English.

genius.d: Someone possessing extraordinary intelligence or skill; especially somebody who has demonstrated this by a creative or original work in science, music, art etc.
genius.e: I think that would qualify as not smart, but genius... and a very stable genius at that!

genuine.d: Not counterfeit, spurious, false, or adulterated
genuine.e: a genuine text;  a genuine production;  genuine materials; genuine friendship

genuinely.d: In a genuine manner; truthfully, truly.
genuinely.e: I thought they were joking at first, but it seemed they were genuinely interested in the talk about glaciers.

gesture.d: A motion of the limbs or body, especially one made to emphasize speech.
gesture.e: The middle-finger gesture is really a nonverbal swear.

gig.d: A performing engagement by a musical group; or, generally, any job or role, especially for a musician or performer.
gig.e: Hey, when are we gonna get that hotel gig again?

globalization.d: The process of becoming a more interconnected world.
globalization.e: Globalization has connected economies and cultures worldwide.

globe.e: the globe of the eye; the globe of a lamp

golden.d: Made of, or relating to, gold.

goodness.d: The state or characteristic of being good.
goodness.e: Thank goodness that the war is over!

governor.d: The chief executive officer of a first-level division of a country.
governor.e: The governor inaugurated the new medical college.

graphic.d: Having a texture that resembles writing, commonly created by exsolution, devitrification and immiscibility processes in igneous rocks.

graphics.d: The art or visual representations displayed by a computer.
graphics.e: This game is really fun, but the graphics aren't very good.

greatly.d: To a great extent or degree.
greatly.e: Expenses greatly exceeded revenues this year.

greenhouse.d: A building used to grow plants, particularly one with large glass windows or plastic sheeting to trap heat from sunlight even in intemperate seasons or climates.

grocery.d: A shop or store that sells groceries; a grocery store.
grocery.e: She went to the grocery store to buy rice and dal.

guideline.d: A non-specific rule or principle that provides direction to action or behaviour.

Once the loan is fully repaid, the lender loses this right of seizure and the assets are then deemed to be unencumbered.

restore.d: To reestablish, or bring back into existence.
