restrict.d: To restrain within boundaries; to limit; to confine
restrict.e: After suffering diahrroea, the patient was restricted to a diet of rice, cold meat, and yoghurt.

restriction.d: The act of restricting, or the state of being restricted.
restriction.e: There are restrictions on plastic use in the city.

retail.d: The sale of goods directly to the consumer, encompassing the storefronts, mail-order, websites, etc., and the corporate mechanisms, branding, advertising, etc. that support them.

retirement.d: The state of being retired; seclusion.
retirement.e: He plans to travel around India after his retirement.

revenue.d: The income returned by an investment.
revenue.e: The state earns significant revenue from tourism.

ridiculous.e: It's ridiculous to charge so much for a little souvenir.

risky.e: Investing in this start-up company could be risky.

rival.d: A competitor (person, team, company, etc.) with the same goal as another, or striving to attain the same thing. Defeating a rival may be a primary or necessary goal of a competitor.
rival.e: Chris is my biggest rival in the 400-metre race.

rob.d: To steal from, especially using force or violence.
rob.e: He robbed three banks before he was caught.

robbery.d: The act or practice of robbing.
robbery.e: The bank robbery was reported in all the newspapers.

romance.d: An adventure, or series of extraordinary events, resembling those narrated in romances.

rose.d: A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.

roughly.d: In a rough manner; without kindness, softness, or gentleness.
roughly.e: Roughly half of the population lives in rural areas.

ruin.d: To cause the fiscal ruin of.
ruin.e: With all these purchases, you surely mean to ruin us!

satisfaction.d: A fulfilment of a need or desire.
satisfaction.e: He enjoyed the dish with great satisfaction. He'll order it again the next time he arrives.

scandal.d: An incident or event that disgraces or damages the reputation of the persons or organization involved.
scandal.e: Their affair was reported as a scandal by most tabloids.

scare.d: To frighten, terrify, startle, especially in a minor way.
scare.e: Did it scare you when I said "Boo!"?

scenario.d: An outline of the plot of a dramatic or literary work.
scenario.e: In the worst-case scenario, the project may be delayed by a month.

scholar.d: A student; one who studies at school or college, typically having a scholarship.
scholar.e: The scholar received a fellowship for her research in Sanskrit.

scratch.d: To mark a surface with a sharp object, thereby leaving a scratch (noun).
scratch.e: A real diamond can easily scratch a pane of glass.

screening.d: Mesh material that is used to screen (as in a "screen door").
screening.e: I'll have to buy some screening and fix the doors before mosquito season starts.

seeker.d: Especially, a religious seeker: a pilgrim, or one who aspires to enlightenment or salvation.
seeker.e: The job seeker submitted her resume to several companies.

seminar.d: A class held for advanced studies in which students meet regularly to discuss original research, under the guidance of a professor.
seminar.e: The university organised a seminar on climate change.

settler.d: Someone who settles in a new location, especially one who takes up residence in a previously uninhabited place; a colonist.
settler.e: the first settlers of New England

spill.d: To spread out or fall out, as above.
spill.e: Some sticky juice spilled onto the kitchen floor.

spite.d: Ill will or hatred toward another, accompanied with the desire to irritate, annoy, or thwart; a want to disturb or put out another; mild malice
spite.e: He was so filled with spite for his ex-wife, he could not hold down a job.

spoil.d: Of food, to become bad, sour or rancid; to decay.
spoil.e: Make sure you put the milk back in the fridge, otherwise it will spoil.

spokesman.d: One who speaks as the voice of a group of people.
spokesman.e: The government spokesman addressed the media.

spokesperson.d: A person who acts as the voice of a group of people.
spokesperson.e: The government spokesperson addressed the media.

spokeswoman.d: A woman who speaks as the voice of a group of people.
spokeswoman.e: The spokeswoman clarified the company's position on the issue.

sponsorship.d: The state or practice of being a sponsor.
sponsorship.e: I find that for me, my sponsorship of a child via a charitable NGO provides a sense of great satisfaction.

sporting.e: He got a job in a sporting goods store.

stall.d: A compartment for a single animal in a stable or cattle shed.
stall.e: His encounters with security, reception, the secretary, and the assistant were all stalls until the general manager's attorney arrived.

stance.d: The manner, pose, or posture in which one stands.
stance.e: The fencer’s stance showed he was ready to begin.

starve.d: To deprive of nourishment or of some vital component.
starve.e: The patient's brain was starved of oxygen.

steadily.e: The economy has been growing steadily over the past five years.

steam.d: The vapor formed when water changes from liquid phase to gas phase.
steam.e: After three weeks in bed he was finally able to sit up under his own steam.

stimulate.d: To arouse an organism to functional activity.
stimulate.e: Reading books can stimulate creativity and imagination.

strengthen.d: To make strong or stronger; to add strength to; to increase the strength of; to fortify.

strictly.d: In a narrow or limited sense.
strictly.e: That term refers strictly to smaller birds.

stroke.d: An act of stroking (moving one's hand over a surface).
stroke.e: She gave the cat a stroke.

stunning.e: The stunning jolt from the taser gun made the criminal stop fleeing.

subsequent.d: Following in time; coming or being after something else at any time, indefinitely.
subsequent.e: Growth was dampened by a softening of the global economy in 2001, but picked up in the subsequent years due to strong growth in China.

subsequently.d: Following, afterwards in either time or place.
subsequently.e: She graduated and subsequently joined a multinational company.

suburb.d: Any subdivision of a conurbation, not necessarily on the periphery.
suburb.e: They live in a quiet suburb on the outskirts of the city.

suffering.d: The condition of someone who suffers; a state of pain or distress.

sufficient.d: Equal to the end proposed; adequate to what is needed; enough
sufficient.e: There is not sufficient access to the internet in many small rural villages.

sufficiently.e: We wanted to build a tepee, but we couldn't find sufficiently long sticks.

surgeon.d: One who performs surgery; a doctor who performs operations on people or animals.
surgeon.e: The surgeon refused to operate because the patient was her son.

survival.d: The fact or act of surviving; continued existence or life.
survival.e: His survival in the open ocean was a miracle; he had fully expected to die.

survivor.d: One who survives, especially one who survives a traumatic experience.
survivor.e: From this moment on, I choose to live not as a victim, but as a survivor.

suspend.d: To hold in an undetermined or undecided state.
suspend.e: to suspend one's judgement or one's disbelief

sustainable.d: Able to be produced or sustained for an indefinite period without damaging the environment, or without depleting a resource; renewable.
sustainable.e: The government promotes sustainable farming practices.

swallow.d: To cause (food, drink etc.) to pass from the mouth into the stomach; to take into the stomach through the throat.
swallow.e: My throat was so sore that I was unable to swallow.

sympathetic.d: Relating to the sympathetic nervous system

tackle.d: To face or deal with, attempting to overcome or fight down.
tackle.e: The government's measures to tackle crime were insufficient.

tag.d: To remove dung tags from a sheep.
tag.e: Regularly tag the rear ends of your sheep.

tap.d: To draw off liquid from a vessel.
tap.e: He tapped a new barrel of beer.

technological.d: Of, relating to, or involving technology, especially modern scientific technology.
technological.e: India has made significant technological advancements.

teens.d: The numbers between thirteen and nineteen.
teens.e: The number of people at the meeting was in the teens.

temple.d: Any place seen as an important centre for some activity.
temple.e: a temple of commerce, a temple of drinking and dining

temporarily.d: For a limited period of time; not permanently.
temporarily.e: The office was temporarily closed for renovation.

tendency.d: A likelihood of behaving in a particular way or going in a particular direction; a tending toward.
tendency.e: Denim has a tendency to fade.

tension.d: The condition of being held in a state between two or more forces, which are acting in opposition to each other.

terminal.d: A computer program that emulates a physical terminal.

terms.d: Any value (variable or constant) or expression separated from another term by a space or an appropriate character, in an overall expression or table.
terms.e: All the terms of this sum cancel out.

terribly.d: So as to cause terror or awe.

treasure.d: A collection of valuable things; accumulated wealth; a stock of money, jewels, etc.

tribe.d: A socially, ethnically, or politically cohesive group of people.

trigger.d: To spark a response, especially a negative emotional response, in (someone).
trigger.e: This story contains a rape scene and may be triggering for rape victims.

trillion.d: (short scale) A million million: 1 followed by twelve zeros, 1012.

troop.d: (collective) A collection of people; a number; a multitude (in general).

tsunami.d: A large and generally unstoppable surge.
tsunami.e: The 2004 tsunami caused widespread devastation along the coastline.

ultimate.d: Being the greatest possible; maximum; most extreme.

unacceptable.e: Bullying in schools is completely unacceptable.

uncertainty.d: A parameter that measures the dispersion of a range of measured values.
uncertainty.e: There was uncertainty about the outcome of the election.

undergo.d: To experience; to pass through a phase.
undergo.e: The project is undergoing great changes.

undertake.d: To commit oneself (to an obligation, activity etc.).
undertake.e: He undertook to exercise more in future.

unfold.d: To release from a fold or pen.

unfortunate.d: Marked or accompanied by or resulting in misfortune
unfortunate.e: It was unfortunate that the match was cancelled due to rain.

unite.e: I hope this song can unite people from all different cultures.

unity.d: A single undivided thing, seen as complete in itself.
unity.e: Unity in diversity is a core value of Indian society.

urgent.e: An urgent appeal was sent out for assistance.

usage.d: The ways and contexts in which spoken and written words are used, determined by a lexicographer's intuition or from corpus analysis.
usage.e: Reduce the usage of plastic bags to protect the environment.

useless.d: Without use or possibility to be used.
useless.e: This fork has prongs that are bent. It's useless now.

valid.e: I will believe him as soon as he offers a valid answer.

variation.e: All of his soups are variations on a single recipe.

vertical.d: Of or pertaining to vertical markets.
vertical.e: We offer specialised accounting software targeting various verticals.

viewpoint.d: The position from which something is observed or considered; an angle, outlook or point of view.
viewpoint.e: The article presents the issue from multiple viewpoints.

visa.d: A permit to enter and leave a country, normally issued by the authorities of the country to be visited.

visible.e: When the sun rises, the world becomes visible.

voluntary.e: a voluntary church, in distinction from an established or state church

voting.d: Action of the verb to vote
voting.e: Voting, in and of itself, is not evil. But when there are only evils to choose from, we need to seek another choice. ― Justin Deschamps

wander.d: To move without purpose or specified destination; often in search of livelihood.

weekly.d: Happening once a week, or every week.
weekly.e: He's going for his weekly check-up at the hospital.

weird.d: Having an unusually strange character or behaviour.
weird.e: There are lots of weird people in this place.

welfare.d: Health, safety, happiness and prosperity; well-being in any respect.

wheat.d: Any of several cereal grains, of the genus Triticum, that yields flour as used in bakery.

whoever.d: Regardless of the person or persons that.
whoever.e: Whoever stole the painting, the police will catch the thief in no time.

widespread.d: Affecting a large area (e.g. the entire land or body); broad in extent; widely diffused.
widespread.e: The use of smartphones is widespread in India.

wisdom.d: An element of personal character that enables one to distinguish the wise from the unwise.
wisdom.e: My grandmother is known for her wisdom and kindness.

withdraw.d: To take back (a comment, etc); retract.

workforce.d: All the workers employed by a specific organization or state, or on a specific project
workforce.e: Women now make up a significant part of the workforce.

workplace.e: Safety measures should be followed in every workplace.

workshop.d: A room, especially one which is not particularly large, used for manufacturing or other light industrial work.

worm.d: A generally tubular invertebrate of the annelid phylum; an earthworm.
worm.e: Don't try to run away, you little worm!

wrist.d: The complex joint between forearm bones, carpus, and metacarpals where the hand is attached to the arm; the carpus in a narrow sense.

abolish.d: To end a law, system, institution, custom or practice.
abolish.e: Slavery was abolished in the nineteenth century.

abortion.e: The law regulates the medical procedure of abortion.

absence.d: A state of being away or withdrawn from a place or from companionship; the period of being away.
absence.e: Absence makes the heart grow fonder.

absent.d: Being away from a place; withdrawn from a place; not present; missing.
absent.e: The part was rudimental or absent.

absurd.d: Contrary to reason or propriety; obviously and flatly opposed to manifest truth; inconsistent with the plain dictates of common sense; logically contradictory; nonsensical; ridiculous; silly.
absurd.e: The idea of cancelling the festival seemed absurd to everyone.

abundance.d: Frequency, amount, ratio of something within a given environment or sample.
abundance.e: India has an abundance of natural resources.

abuse.d: To put to a wrong use; to misapply; to use improperly; to misuse; to use for a wrong purpose or end; to pervert

academy.d: A school or place of training in which some special art is taught.
academy.e: the military academy at West Point; a riding academy; the Academy of Music.; a music academy; a language academy

accelerate.d: To quicken the natural or ordinary progression or process of.
accelerate.e: to accelerate the growth of a plant, the increase of wealth, etc.

acceptance.d: The act of accepting; a receiving of something offered, with acquiescence, approbation, or satisfaction; especially, favourable reception; approval.
acceptance.e: the acceptance of a gift, office, doctrine, etc.

accomplishment.d: The act of accomplishing; completion; fulfilment.
accomplishment.e: the accomplishment of an enterprise, of a prophecy, etc

accordance.e: The work was done in accordance with the safety guidelines.

accordingly.e: The schedule was changed, and the team adjusted accordingly.

accountability.d: The state of being accountable; liability to be called on to render an account; accountableness; responsible for; answerable for.
accountability.e: Accountability is essential in any democratic institution.

accountable.d: Obliged, when called upon, to answer (for one’s deeds); answerable.
accountable.e: Every man is accountable to God for his conduct.

accumulate.d: To heap up in a mass; to pile up; to collect or bring together (either literally or figuratively)
accumulate.e: He wishes to accumulate a sum of money.

accumulation.d: The process of growing into a heap or a large amount.
accumulation.e: an accumulation of earth, of sand, of evils, of wealth, or of honors

accusation.d: A formal charge brought against a person in a court of law.
accusation.e: He denied the accusation of wrongdoing.

accused.d: The person charged with an offense; the defendant in a criminal case.

acquisition.d: The act or process of acquiring.
acquisition.e: The acquisition of sports equipment can be fun in itself.

acre.d: Any of various similar units of area in other systems.
acre.e: I like my new house - there’s acres of space!

activation.d: The process of making a radioisotope by bombarding a stable element with neutrons or protons.
activation.e: The activation of the new system will take place next week.

apparatus.d: The entirety of means whereby a specific production is made existent or task accomplished.
apparatus.e: The laboratory has all the necessary apparatus for experiments.

appetite.d: The desire for some personal gratification, either of the body or of the mind.

applaud.d: To express approval (of something) by clapping the hands.
applaud.e: After the performance, the audience applauded for five minutes.

applicable.e: The discount is applicable only to online purchases.

appoint.d: To set, fix or determine (a time or place for something such as a meeting, or the meeting itself) by authority or agreement.

appreciation.d: A fair valuation or estimate of merit, worth, weight, etc.; recognition of excellence.

arbitrary.d: Any, out of all that are possible.

architectural.d: Resembling architecture in style; seeming to have been designed (by an architect).

archive.d: A place for storing earlier, and often historical, material. An archive usually contains documents (letters, records, newspapers, etc.) or other types of media kept for historical interest.
archive.e: His archive of Old High German texts is the most extensive in Britain.

arena.d: The sand-covered centre of an amphitheatre where contests were held in Ancient Rome.

arguably.d: As can be supported or proven by sound logical deduction, evidence, and precedent.
arguably.e: She is arguably the best batsman in women's cricket.

articulate.e: I wish he’d articulate his words more clearly.

ash.d: The solid remains of a fire.

aspiration.d: A burst of air that follows the release of some consonants.
aspiration.e: Morgan has an aspiration of winning the game.

aspire.d: To have a strong desire or ambition to achieve something.
aspire.e: He aspires to become a successful doctor.

assassination.d: The murder of a person, especially for political reasons or for personal gain.
assassination.e: The assassination of the king occurred at night.

assault.e: Loud music assaulted our ears as we entered the building.

assemble.e: The parents assembled in the school hall.

assembly.d: A set of pieces that work together in unison as a mechanism or device.

assert.d: To use or exercise and thereby prove the existence of.

assertion.d: Something which is asserted; a declaration; a statement asserted.
assertion.e: You're a man of strong assertions!

asylum.d: The protection, physical and legal, afforded by such a place.
asylum.e: The country granted asylum to the political refugees.

atrocity.d: An extremely cruel act; a horrid act of injustice.
atrocity.e: The regime is guilty of mass atrocities including forced displacement and the use of chemical weapons.

attain.d: To gain (an object or desired result).
attain.e: To attain such a high level of proficiency requires hours of practice each day.

attendance.d: The count or list of individuals present for an event.
attendance.e: The class sat down so that the teacher could take attendance.

attorney.d: A lawyer; one who advises or represents others in legal matters as a profession.

attribute.d: To associate ownership or authorship of (something) to someone.
attribute.e: This poem is attributed to Browning.

authentic.d: Conforming to reality and therefore worthy of trust, reliance, or belief.

authorize.d: To grant (someone) the permission or power necessary to do (something).
authorize.e: The General Assembly authorized the Council to take up the matter.

autonomy.d: The capacity to make an informed, uncoerced decision.
autonomy.e: The state enjoys a certain degree of autonomy in governance.

availability.e: What is your availability this week?

await.d: To be in store for; to be ready or in waiting for.

backdrop.d: A decorated cloth hung at the back of a stage.

backing.e: It's a volunteer organization that works with backing from the city and a few grants.

backup.e: If the goalkeeper is injured, we have a backup.

bail.d: Security, usually a sum of money, exchanged for the release of an arrested person as a guarantee of that person's appearance for trial.

ballot.d: Originally, a small ball placed in a container to cast a vote; now, by extension, a piece of paper or card used for this purpose, or some other means used to signify a vote.

banner.d: A flag or standard used by a military commander, monarch or nation.
banner.e: The mayor hung a banner across Main Street to commemorate the town's 100th anniversary.

bare.d: Having had what usually covers (something) removed.
bare.e: The trees were left bare after the swarm of locusts devoured all the leaves.

barrel.d: A round vessel or cask, of greater length than breadth, and bulging in the middle, made of staves bound with hoops, and having flat ends or heads. Sometimes applied to a similar cylindrical container made of metal, usually called a drum.

battlefield.d: The area where a land battle is or was fought, which is not necessarily a field.
battlefield.e: 1886 The night of the 16th of May found McPherson's command bivouacked from two to six miles west of the battlefield, along the line of the road to Vicksburg — Ulysses S. Grant, Personal memoirs of U.S. Grant, Chapter 35.

bay.d: Laurus nobilis, a tree or shrub of the family Lauraceae, having dark green leaves and berries.

beam.d: Any large piece of timber or iron long in proportion to its thickness, and prepared for use.
beam.e: This ship has more beam than that one.

beast.d: Anything regarded as larger or more powerful than one of its normal size or strength.
beast.e: That is a beast of a stadium.

bench.d: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.
bench.e: They sat on a park bench and tossed bread crumbs to the ducks and pigeons.

benchmark.d: A standard by which something is evaluated or measured.

beneath.d: In a position that is lower in rank, dignity, etc.
beneath.e: The treasure was hidden beneath the old banyan tree.

beneficiary.d: One who benefits or receives an advantage.
beneficiary.e: You are the lucky beneficiary of this special offer.

betray.d: To deliver into the hands of an enemy by treachery or fraud, in violation of trust; to give up treacherously or faithlessly.

bind.d: To exert a binding or restraining influence.

biography.d: A person's life story, especially one published.
biography.e: There are many biographies of Benjamin Franklin.

bishop.d: An overseer of congregations: either any such overseer, generally speaking, or (in Eastern Orthodoxy, Catholicism, Anglicanism, etc.) an official in the church hierarchy (actively or nominally) governing a diocese, supervising the church's priests, deacons, and property in its territory.

bizarre.d: Strangely unconventional in style or appearance.
bizarre.e: The bizarre weather pattern surprised everyone.

blade.d: The sharp cutting edge of a knife, chisel, or other tool, a razor blade/sword.

blast.d: To open up a hole in, usually by means of a sudden and imprecise method (such as an explosion).

blend.d: To mingle; to mix; to unite intimately; to pass or shade insensibly into each other.
blend.e: To make hummus you need to blend chickpeas, olive oil, lemon juice and garlic.

bless.d: To make something holy by religious rite, sanctify.
bless.e: The elders blessed the newlywed couple.

blessing.e: After two weeks of sun, last night's rainfall was a blessing.

boast.e: It was his regular boast that he could eat two full English breakfasts in one sitting.

bonus.d: Something extra that is good; an added benefit.
bonus.e: The employee of the week receives a bonus for his excellent work.

boom.d: A low-pitched, resonant sound, such as of an explosion.

bounce.d: To change the direction of motion after hitting an obstacle.
bounce.e: The tennis ball bounced off the wall before coming to rest in the ditch.

boundary.d: The dividing line or location between two areas.
boundary.e: The river forms the boundary between the two states.

breach.e: They breached the outer wall, but not the main one.

breakdown.d: A physical collapse or lapse of mental stability
breakdown.e: After so much stress, he suffered a breakdown and simply gave up.

breakthrough.d: An advance through and past enemy lines.
breakthrough.e: Albert Einstein is credited with making some of the greatest breakthroughs in modern physics.

breed.d: To arrange the mating of specific animals.
breed.e: She wanted to breed her cow to the neighbor's registered bull.

broadband.d: A wide band of electromagnetic frequencies

browser.d: A person or animal who browses.
browser.e: Update your browser for a better browsing experience.

brutal.d: Savagely violent, vicious, ruthless, or cruel
brutal.e: The summer heat in Delhi can be brutal.

buck.d: A male deer, antelope, sheep, goat, rabbit, hare, and sometimes the male of other animals such as the hamster, ferret and shad.

buddy.e: They have been buddies since they were in school.

bulk.e: I understood the bulk of what you were saying, just one of two points I need to hear again.

burden.d: A heavy load that is difficult to carry; a duty or responsibility that causes worry.
burden.e: He didn't want to be a burden on his family.

bureaucracy.d: The body of officers and administrators, especially of a government.
bureaucracy.e: The bureaucracy is expanding to meet the needs of the expanding bureaucracy. (apocryphal quip)

burial.e: The burial ceremony was attended by close family members.

burst.d: To produce as an effect of bursting.
burst.e: to burst a hole through the wall

cabinet.d: A storage closet either separate from, or built into, a wall.
cabinet.e: The kitchen cabinet was filled with spices and condiments.

calculation.d: The act or process of calculating.
calculation.e: By my calculation, we should be there by midnight.

canvas.d: A type of coarse cloth, woven from hemp, useful for making sails and tents or as a surface for paintings.
canvas.e: The author takes rural midwestern life as a canvas for a series of tightly woven character studies.

capability.d: The power or ability to generate an outcome
capability.e: The new software has advanced data processing capabilities.

capitalism.d: A socio-economic system based on private ownership of resources or capital.
capitalism.e: Capitalism has shaped the modern global economy.

cargo.d: Freight carried by a ship, aircraft, or motor vehicle.
cargo.e: The ship carried cargo from Mumbai to Dubai.

carriage.d: A wheeled vehicle, generally drawn by horse power.
carriage.e: The carriage ride was very romantic.

carve.d: To cut meat in order to serve it.
carve.e: You carve the roast and I'll serve the vegetables.

casino.d: A public building or room for entertainment, especially gambling.
casino.e: Goa is known for its casinos and beach resorts.

casualty.d: A person suffering from injuries or who has been killed due to an accident or through an act of violence.
casualty.e: The hospital reported no casualties after the minor earthquake.

catalogue.d: A systematic list of names, books, pictures etc.

cattle.d: Domesticated bovine animals (cows, bulls, steers etc).
cattle.e: Do you want to raise cattle?

caution.d: One who draws attention or causes astonishment by their behaviour.
caution.e: Oh, that boy, he's a caution! He does make me laugh.

cautious.d: Careful; using or exercising caution; tentative
cautious.e: He took a few cautious steps toward the cave.

cease.e: And with that, he ceased twitching.

cemetery.d: A place where the dead are buried; a graveyard or memorial park.
cemetery.e: The old cemetery is located on the outskirts of the town.

chamber.d: A room or set of rooms, particularly:
chamber.e: The resolution, which speedily passed the Senate, was unable to gain a majority in the lower chamber.

chaos.d: Any state of disorder; a confused or amorphous mixture or conglomeration.
chaos.e: After the earthquake, the local hospital was in chaos

characterize.d: To depict someone or something a particular way (often negative).

charm.d: An object, act or words believed to have magic power (usually carries a positive connotation).

charter.d: A document issued by some authority, creating a public or private institution, and defining its purposes and privileges.
charter.e: 2005 November 30, Stephen Foley "The market where 'caveat emptor' has become a charter for fraud" The Independent, London

chronic.d: Of a problem, that continues over an extended period of time.
chronic.e: chronic unemployment; chronic poverty; chronic anger; chronic life

chunk.d: A part of something that has been separated.

circulate.d: To move in circles or through a circuit

circulation.d: Currency; circulating coins; notes, bills, etc., current for coin.
circulation.e: The new bills will come into circulation next Friday.

citizenship.d: The status of being a citizen, in its various senses.

eligible.d: Allowed to and meeting the necessary conditions required to participate in or be chosen for something
eligible.e: Students above eighteen years of age are eligible to vote.

elite.d: A special group or social class of people which have a superior intellectual, social or economic status as, the elite of society.
elite.e: The elite group of scientists worked on the space mission.

embark.d: To get on a boat or ship or (outside the USA) an aeroplane.

embarrassment.d: A person or thing which is the cause of humiliation to another.
embarrassment.e: Kevin, you are an embarrassment to this family.

embassy.d: The function or duty of an ambassador.
embassy.e: The American embassy to France is located in Paris.

embed.d: To encapsulate within another document or data file.
embed.e: The instructions showed how to embed a chart from the spreadsheet within the wordprocessor document.

embody.d: To represent in some other form, such as a code of laws.
embody.e: The US Constitution aimed to embody the ideals of diverse groups of people, from Puritans to Deists.

emergence.d: In particular: the arising of emergent structure in complex systems.
emergence.e: The emergence of online learning changed education in India.

empirical.d: Pertaining to or based on experience.
empirical.e: The theory was supported by empirical evidence.

empower.d: To give someone more confidence and/or strength to do something, often by enabling them to increase their control over their own life or situation.
empower.e: John found that starting up his own business empowered him greatly in social situations.

enact.e: The government plans to enact a new consumer protection law.

encompass.d: To go around, especially, to circumnavigate.

encouragement.e: Her teacher's encouragement helped her overcome challenges.

encouraging.e: Although there is no cure for the disease yet, the research results are encouraging.

endeavour.d: A sincere attempt; a determined or assiduous effort towards a specific goal; assiduous or persistent activity.

endless.d: Having or seeming to have no end or limit.

endorse.d: To support, to back, to give one's approval to, especially officially or by signature.
endorse.e: The athlete endorsed a popular sportswear brand.

endorsement.d: The act or quality of endorsing
endorsement.e: Companies sometimes pay millions for product endorsement by celebrities.

endure.d: To continue or carry on, despite obstacles or hardships; to persist.
endure.e: The singer's popularity endured for decades.

enforce.d: To keep up, impose or bring into effect something, not necessarily by force.
enforce.e: The police are there to enforce the law.

enforcement.d: That which enforces, constraints, gives force, authority, or effect to; constraint; force applied.
enforcement.e: Strict enforcement of traffic rules reduced accidents.

engagement.d: An appointment, especially to speak or perform.
engagement.e: The lecturer has three speaking engagements this week.

engaging.d: That engages the attention; engrossing, interesting; enthralling.
engaging.e: I found the first of the Harry Potter books a very engaging read.

enquire.e: He enquired about the availability of rental bicycles in the town.

enrich.d: To make (someone or something) rich or richer.

enrol.d: To enlist (someone) or make (someone) a member of
enrol.e: They were eager to enroll new recruits.

ensue.d: To occur afterwards, as a result or effect.
ensue.e: Give three freshmen six bottles of wine, and hilarity will ensue.

enterprise.d: A company, business, organization, or other purposeful endeavor.
enterprise.e: A micro-enterprise is defined as a business having 5 or fewer employees and a low seed capital.

enthusiast.d: A person filled with or guided by enthusiasm.
enthusiast.e: He is a cricket enthusiast who never misses a match.

entitle.d: To give power or authority (to do something).
entitle.e: A passport entitles the bearer to travel to other countries.

entity.d: The state or quality of being or existence.
entity.e: The group successfully maintains its tribal entity.

epidemic.d: A widespread disease that affects many individuals in a population.

equality.e: The Constitution guarantees equality for all citizens.

equation.d: The act or process of equating two or more things, or the state of those things being equal (that is, identical).
equation.e: We need to bring the balance of power into equation

erect.d: To put up by the fitting together of materials or parts.
erect.e: to erect a house or a fort

escalate.d: In technical support, to transfer a customer, a problem, etc. to the next higher level of authority
escalate.e: The tech 1 escalated the caller to a tech 2.

essence.d: The concentrated form of a plant or drug obtained through a distillation process.

establishment.d: The act of establishing; a ratifying or ordaining; settlement; confirmation.
establishment.e: Since their establishment of the company in 1984, they have grown into a global business.

eternal.d: Exceedingly great or bad; used as an intensifier.

evacuate.d: To cause to leave or withdraw from.
evacuate.e: The firefighters decided to evacuate all the inhabitants from the street.

evoke.d: To cause the manifestation of something (emotion, picture, etc.) in someone's mind or imagination.
evoke.e: Being here evokes long forgotten memories.

evolutionary.d: Of or relating to the biological theory of evolution.
evolutionary.e: The evolutionary history of marine mammals includes land-dwelling ancestors.

exaggerate.d: To overstate, to describe more than is fact.
exaggerate.e: He said he'd slept with hundreds of girls, but I know he's exaggerating. The real number is about ten.

excellence.e: The school is known for academic excellence.

exceptional.d: Corresponding to something of lower dimension under a birational correspondence.
exceptional.e: an exceptional curve; an exceptional divisor

excess.d: The degree or amount by which one thing or number exceeds another; remainder.
excess.e: The difference between two numbers is the excess of one over the other.

exclusion.d: An item not covered by an insurance policy.
exclusion.e: The exclusion of any community is against democratic values.

exclusive.d: Excluding items or members that do not meet certain conditions.
exclusive.e: Exclusive clubs tend to serve exclusive brands of food and drinks, in the same exorbitant price range, such as the 'finest' French châteaux.

exclusively.d: (focus) to the exclusion of anything or anyone else; solely or entirely
exclusively.e: The shop sells exclusively organic products.

execute.d: To kill as punishment for capital crimes.
execute.e: There are certain states where it is lawful to execute prisoners convicted of certain crimes.

execution.e: The mission's successful execution lifted the troops' morale.

exert.e: I exerted myself in today's training.

exile.d: Someone who is banished from their home or country.

expenditure.e: The expenditure of time, money, and political capital on this project has been excessive.

experimental.d: Pertaining to or founded on experiment.

expire.e: My library card will expire next week.

explicit.e: I gave explicit instructions for him to stay here, but he followed me, anyway.

explicitly.e: The teacher explicitly stated the deadline for the assignment.

exploitation.d: The improper use of something for selfish purposes.
exploitation.e: the exploitation of children in beauty pageants

explosive.d: With the capability to, or likely to, explode.

extremist.d: A person who holds extreme views, especially one who advocates such views; a radical or fanatic.

facilitate.e: The workshop aims to facilitate learning among young professionals.

faction.d: A form of literature, film etc., that treats real people or events as if they were fiction; a mix of fact and fiction
faction.e: A faction within the party disagreed with the decision.

faculty.e: She transferred from the Faculty of Science to the Faculty of Medicine.

fade.d: To sink away; to disappear gradually; to grow dim; to vanish.
fade.e: The milkman's whistling faded into the distance.

fairness.d: The property of being fair or equitable.
fairness.e: Some questioned the fairness of the new laws.

fatal.d: Causing a sudden end to the running of a program.
fatal.e: a fatal error; a fatal exception

fate.d: The presumed cause, force, principle, or divine will that predetermines events.

favourable.d: Convenient or at a suitable time; opportune.
favourable.e: The rain stopped at a favourable time for our tennis match.

feat.d: A relatively rare or difficult accomplishment.

feminist.d: A member of a feminist political movement
feminist.e: She is a feminist who advocates for equal opportunities.

fibre.d: A single piece of a given material, elongated and roughly round in cross-section, often twisted with other fibres to form thread.
fibre.e: The microscope showed several different fibres stuck to the sole of the shoe.

fierce.d: Exceedingly violent, severe, ferocious, cruel or savage.
fierce.e: A fierce storm battered the coast.

film-maker.d: A producer or director of films/movies.
film-maker.e: The Indian film-maker won an award at the Cannes festival.

filter.d: To pass through a filter or to act as though passing through a filter.
filter.e: The water filtered through the rock and soil.

firearm.d: A personal weapon that uses explosive powder to propel a projectile often made of lead.
firearm.e: The police recovered a firearm from the crime scene.

fixture.d: Something that is fixed in place, especially a permanent appliance or other item of personal property that is considered part of a house and is sold with it; compare fitting, furnishing.

flawed.e: Flawed diamonds are generally not used in jewellery.

flee.e: The prisoner tried to flee, but was caught by the guards.

fleet.d: A group of vessels or vehicles.

flesh.d: The soft tissue of the body, especially muscle and fat.

flexibility.e: I had some flexibility in terms of whether to stay in a hotel or in a bed-and-breakfast.

flourish.e: The barley flourished in the warm weather.

fluid.d: Any substance which can flow with relative ease, tends to assume the shape of its container, and obeys Bernoulli's principle; a liquid, gas or plasma.

footage.d: An amount of film or tape that has been used to record something.
footage.e: The footage we shot at the riot yesterday got ruined.

foreigner.d: A person from a foreign country.
foreigner.e: The foreigner was fascinated by the colourful Indian markets.

forge.d: To create a forgery of; to make a counterfeit item of; to copy or imitate unlawfully.
forge.e: He had to forge his ex-wife's signature.  The jury learned the documents had been forged.

formula.d: A formulation; a prescription; a mixture or solution made in a prescribed manner; the identity and quantities of ingredients of such a mixture.
formula.e: The formula of the rocket fuel has not been revealed.

formulate.d: To reduce to, or express in, a formula; to put in a clear and definite form of statement or expression.
formulate.e: The committee was formed to formulate a new policy.

forth.d: Out into view; from a particular place or position.
forth.e: The plants in spring put forth leaves.

forthcoming.d: Approaching or about to take place.
forthcoming.e: I shall vote in the forthcoming election.

foster.e: Our company fosters an appreciation for the arts.

fragile.d: Easily broken or destroyed, and thus often of subtle or intricate structure.
fragile.e: He is a very fragile person and gets easily depressed.

franchise.d: The authorization granted by a company to sell or distribute its goods or services in a certain area.

frankly.d: (manner) In a frank, open or (too) honest manner.
frankly.e: He spoke frankly about the economy.

frustrating.d: Discouraging; causing annoyance or anger by excessive difficulty

frustration.d: The act of frustrating, or the state, or an instance of being frustrated
frustration.e: He expressed his frustration over the delayed train.

functional.d: Only for functional purposes, notably in architecture
functional.e: A functional construction element generally must meet higher technical but lower aesthetical requirements

fundraising.d: The process of collecting money by requesting donations from individuals and businesses.
fundraising.e: The school organised a fundraising event for the charity.

funeral.d: A ceremony to honour and remember a deceased person. Often distinguished from a memorial service by the presence of the body of the deceased.
funeral.e: Many mourners turned up at the local artist's funeral to pay homage.

gallon.d: 231 cubic inches or approximately 3.785 liters for liquids (a "U.S. liquid gallon")
gallon.e: The pipe burst and gallons of water flooded into the kitchen.

gambling.d: An activity characterised by a balance between winning and losing that is governed by a mixture of skill and chance, usually with money wagered on the outcome.

gathering.d: A meeting or get-together; a party or social function.
gathering.e: I met her at a gathering of engineers and scientists.

gaze.e: In fact, for Antonioni this gazing is probably the most fundamental of all cognitive activities ... (from Thinking in the Absence of Image)

gear.d: Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.

generic.d: Very comprehensive; pertaining or appropriate to large classes or groups as opposed to specific.
generic.e: "Shrimp" is the generic name for a number of species of sea creature.

genocide.d: The systematic killing of substantial numbers of people on the basis of their ethnicity, religion, political beliefs, social status, or other particularities.
genocide.e: A genocide will always be followed by the denial that it ever happened.

glance.e: She glanced at her reflection as she passed the mirror.

glimpse.d: A brief look, glance, or peek.
glimpse.e: I only got a glimpse of the car, so I can tell you the colour but not the registration number.

glorious.d: Exhibiting attributes, qualities, or acts that are worthy of or receive glory.

glory.d: Great or overwhelming beauty or splendour.

governance.d: The specific system by which a political system is ruled.
governance.e: Good governance leads to better public services.

grace.e: The Princess brought grace to an otherwise dull and boring party.

grasp.d: That which is accessible; that which is within one's reach or ability.
grasp.e: The goal is within my grasp.

gravity.d: The state or condition of being grave; seriousness.
gravity.e: I hope you appreciate the gravity of the situation.

grid.d: A rectangular array of squares or rectangles of equal size, such as in a crossword puzzle.

grief.d: Pain of mind arising from misfortune, significant personal loss, bereavement, misconduct of oneself or others, etc.; sorrow; sadness.
grief.e: She was worn out from so much grief.

grin.d: To grin as part of producing a particular facial expression, such as a smile or sneer.
grin.e: He grinned a broad smile when I told him the result.

grind.d: To shape with the force of friction.
grind.e: grind a lens; grind an axe

grip.d: To take hold of, particularly with the hand.
grip.e: After a few slips, the tires gripped the pavement.

gross.d: (of behaviour considered to be wrong) Highly or conspicuously offensive.
gross.e: a gross mistake;  gross injustice;  gross negligence; a gross insult

guerrilla.d: A soldier in a small independent group, fighting against the government or regular forces by surprise raids.

guidance.d: Advice or counselling on some topic.

gut.d: The alimentary canal, especially the intestine.

hail.d: Of hail, to fall from the sky.
hail.e: They say it's going to hail tomorrow.

halfway.d: Half of the way between two points; midway.
halfway.e: a halfway decent place to sleep

halt.d: A cessation, either temporary or permanent.
halt.e: The contract negotiations put a halt to operations.

handful.d: Something which can only be managed with difficulty.
handful.e: Those twins are a real handful to look after.

handling.d: A touching, controlling, managing, using, take care of, etc., with the hand or hands, or as with the hands.

handy.e: She's very handy: she made all her own kitchen cupboards.

harassment.d: Persistent attacks and criticism causing worry and distress.
harassment.e: The company has a zero-tolerance policy against harassment.

hardware.d: The part of a computer that is fixed and cannot be altered without replacement or physical modification; motherboard, expansion cards, etc. Compare software.

harmony.d: A literary work which brings together or arranges systematically parallel passages of historians respecting the same events, and shows their agreement or consistency.

harsh.d: Unpleasantly rough to the touch or other senses.

harvest.d: To be occupied bringing in a harvest
harvest.e: Harvesting is a stressing, thirsty occupation

hatred.e: Hatred and intolerance have no place in a civilised society.

haunt.d: To inhabit, or visit frequently (most often used in reference to ghosts).
haunt.e: A couple of ghosts haunt the old, burnt-down house.

hazard.d: The chance of suffering harm; danger, peril, risk of loss.
hazard.e: He encountered the enemy at the hazard of his reputation and life.

heighten.d: To advance, increase, augment, make larger, more intense, stronger etc.
heighten.e: to heighten a flavor or a tint

heritage.d: Having a certain background, such as growing up with a second language.
heritage.e: The university requires heritage Spanish students to enroll in a specially designed Spanish program not available to non-heritage students.

hierarchy.d: A body of authoritative officials organized in nested ranks.
hierarchy.e: The organisation has a clear hierarchy of management.

high-profile.e: The trial was a high-profile case that attracted national attention.

hint.d: To develop and add hints to a font.
hint.e: The typographer worked all day on hinting her new font so it would look good on computer screens.

homeland.d: The country that one regards as home.
homeland.e: He returned to his homeland after living abroad for ten years.

hopeful.d: Feeling or inspiring optimism about a future event.
hopeful.e: She is hopeful about getting the job.

horizon.d: The visible horizontal line or point (in all directions) that appears to connect the Earth to the sky.
horizon.e: A tall building was visible on the horizon.

horn.d: The hard substance from which animals' horns are made, sometimes used by man as a material for making various objects.
horn.e: an umbrella with a handle made of horn

hostage.d: Something that constrains one's actions because it is at risk.
hostage.e: The police negotiated the safe release of the hostages.

hostile.d: Not friendly, appropriate to an enemy; showing the disposition of an enemy; showing ill will and malevolence, or a desire to thwart and injure

hostility.e: My resentment and anger towards you caused hostility and a division between us.

humanitarian.d: Relating to humanism or the humanities.
humanitarian.e: The NGO provides humanitarian aid in disaster-affected regions.

humanity.d: Any academic subject belonging to the humanities.
humanity.e: Philosophy is a humanity while psychology is a science.

humble.d: Not pretentious or magnificent; unpretending; unassuming.
humble.e: He lives in a humble one-bedroom cottage.

hydrogen.d: Molecular hydrogen (H2), a colourless, odourless and flammable gas at room temperature.
hydrogen.e: Hydrogen is the lightest element on the periodic table.

identification.d: The act of identifying, or proving to be the same.
identification.e: Much education and experience is required for proper identification of bird species

ideological.d: Of or pertaining to an ideology.
ideological.e: The two parties have sharp ideological differences.

ideology.d: Doctrine, philosophy, body of beliefs or principles belonging to an individual or group.
ideology.e: A dictatorship, in order to secure its reign, bans things that do not conform to its ideology.

idiot.d: A person of low general intelligence.
idiot.e: He felt like an idiot for forgetting her birthday.

ignorance.d: The condition of being uninformed or uneducated. Lack of knowledge or information.
ignorance.e: Ignorance of the law is not an excuse.

imagery.d: The work of one who makes images or visible representation of objects.
imagery.e: The poem uses vivid imagery to describe the monsoon.

imminent.d: About to happen, occur, or take place very soon, especially of something which won't last long.
imminent.e: Dark clouds signalled that a storm was imminent.

implementation.d: A result of implementing something; a finished product, system or device.
implementation.e: His implementation works, but it needs some fine-tuning.

imprison.d: To put in or as if in prison; confine.
imprison.e: The court decided to imprison the guilty for five years.

imprisonment.d: A confinement in a place, especially a prison or a jail, as punishment for a crime.
imprisonment.e: He was sentenced to ten years of imprisonment.

inability.d: Lack of the ability to do something; incapability.
inability.e: His inability to speak the local language was a challenge.

inadequate.d: Not adequate; not fit for the purpose

inappropriate.d: Not appropriate; not suitable for the situation, time, or place.
inappropriate.e: It is inappropriate to burp at a formal dinner.

incidence.d: The extent or the relative frequency of something happening.
incidence.e: The incidence of dengue increases during the monsoon season.

inclined.d: At an angle to the horizontal; slanted or sloped.
inclined.e: The take-off ramp was inclined at 20 degrees.

inclusion.d: An addition or annex to a group, set, or total.
inclusion.e: The poem was a new inclusion in the textbook.

incur.e: Late payment will incur a penalty fee.

indicator.d: A pointer or index that indicates something.
indicator.e: Rising temperatures are an indicator of climate change.

indictment.d: The official legal document outlining the charges concerned; bill of indictment.
indictment.e: The indictment charged the official with corruption.

indigenous.d: Born or originating in, native to a land or region, especially before an intrusion.
indigenous.e: Many indigenous communities live in the forests of central India.

induce.d: To cause, bring about, lead to.
induce.e: His meditation induced a compromise.   Opium induces sleep.

indulge.d: To satisfy the wishes or whims of.
indulge.e: Grandma indulges the kids with sweets.

inequality.e: The organisation works to reduce economic inequality.

infamous.d: Having a bad reputation, disreputable; notoriously bad, unpleasant or evil; widely known, especially for something bad.

infant.d: A very young human being, from birth to somewhere between six months and two years of age, needing almost constant care and/or attention.

inflict.e: They inflicted terrible pains on her to obtain a confession.

influential.e: Jane was very influential in getting the motion passed.

inherent.d: Naturally as part or consequence of something.
inherent.e: There is an inherent risk in investing in the stock market.

inhibit.d: To hold in or hold back; to keep in check; restrain.
inhibit.e: Fear can inhibit a person from trying new things.

initiate.d: To instruct in the rudiments or principles; to introduce.
initiate.e: The university initiated a mentorship programme for freshers.

inject.d: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
inject.e: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

injection.d: The supply of additional funding to a person or a business.
injection.e: The troubled business received a much-needed cash injection.

injustice.d: Violation of the rights of another person or people.
injustice.e: Silence in the face of gross injustice, or support for it, or even active involvement therein, comes at a price.

inmate.d: A person confined to an institution such as a prison (as a convict) or hospital (as a patient).
inmate.e: The prison provides vocational training to its inmates.

insertion.d: The act of inserting, or something inserted.
insertion.e: The insertion of the key card opens the hotel room door.

insider.d: A person who has special knowledge about the inner workings of a group, organization, or institution.
insider.e: The insider provided crucial information to the investigators.

inspect.e: The general inspected the troops and their barracks.

inspection.d: The act of examining something, often closely.
inspection.e: Upon closer inspection, the animal turned out to be a dolphin, not a shark!

inspiration.d: The act of an elevating or stimulating influence upon the intellect, emotions or creativity.
inspiration.e: She was waiting for inspiration to write a book.

instinct.d: A natural or inherent impulse or behaviour.
instinct.e: Many animals fear fire by instinct.

institutional.d: Of, pertaining to, characteristic of, or organized along the lines of an institution.
institutional.e: Institutional reforms are needed for better governance.

instruct.e: Listen carefully when someone instructs you how to assemble the furniture.

instrumental.d: Pertaining to, made by, or prepared for, an instrument, especially a musical instrument (rather than the human voice).

insufficient.e: Insufficient time is available to reflect on the problem

insult.d: Something that causes offence (for example, by being of an unacceptable quality).
insult.e: The way the orchestra performed tonight was an insult to my ears.

intact.d: Not damaged or impaired in any way; complete.
intact.e: The building survived the earthquake intact.

intake.d: An act or instance of taking in.
intake.e: an intake of oxygen or food

integral.d: Constituting a whole together with other parts or factors; not omittable or removable

integrated.d: Composed and coordinated to form a whole

integration.d: The operation of finding the integral of a function.
integration.e: The integration of technology in education has improved learning.

integrity.d: Steadfast adherence to a strict moral or ethical code.
integrity.e: A leader must act with integrity at all times.

intensity.d: Can mean any of radiant intensity, luminous intensity or irradiance.
intensity.e: The intensity of the summer heat increased this year.

intensive.d: Related to the need to manage life-threatening conditions by means of sophisticated life support and monitoring.
intensive.e: She was moved to the intensive-care unit of the hospital.

intent.d: A purpose; something that is intended.

interface.d: The point of interconnection or contact between entities.
interface.e: Public relations firms often serve as the interface between a company and the press.

interfere.d: To get involved or involve oneself, causing disturbance.
interfere.e: I always try not to interfere with other people’s personal affairs.

interference.d: The illegal obstruction of an opponent in some ball games.
interference.e: They were glued to the TV, as the referee called out a fifteen yard penalty for interference.

interim.d: Relating to or being a temporary arrangement; provisional.
interim.e: An interim report will be submitted next week.

interior.d: Within any limits, enclosure, or substance; inside; internal; inner.
interior.e: the interior apartments of a house; the interior surface of a hollow ball

intermediate.d: Being between two extremes, or in the middle of a range.

intervene.d: To become involved in a situation, so as to alter or prevent an action.
intervene.e: The police had to be called to intervene in the fight.

intervention.d: The action of intervening; interfering in some course of events.
intervention.e: Early medical intervention saved the patient's life.

intimate.d: Very close and familiar; having a warm personal relationship.
intimate.e: He intimated that we should leave before the argument escalated.

intriguing.d: Arousing great curiosity or interest; fascinating.
intriguing.e: The detective found an intriguing clue.

investigator.e: The investigator gathered evidence at the crime scene.

invisible.e: I went invisible so that my ex-girlfriend wouldn't send me instant messages.

invoke.d: To solicit, petition for, appeal to a favorable attitude.
invoke.e: The envoy invoked the King of Kings's magnanimity to reduce his province's tribute after another drought.

involvement.d: The act of involving, or the state of being involved.
involvement.e: Community involvement is key to the success of any social project.

ironic.d: Contrary or opposite to what may be expected.
ironic.e: It's ironic to have a blizzard in the middle of the summer.

ironically.d: (evaluative) Used to draw attention to an ironic aspect of a situation being described.
ironically.e: Ironically, the automated checkout system ended up costing more in repairs than the workers it was meant to replace.

irony.d: A statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is written literally; the use of words expressing something other than their literal intention, often in a humorous context.

irrelevant.d: Not related, not applicable, unimportant, not connected.
irrelevant.e: The question was deemed irrelevant to the topic.

isolation.d: The state of being isolated, detached, or separated.
isolation.e: Social distancing is social isolation, which is a human rights violation on par with torture and other war crimes.

judicial.d: Of or relating to the court system or the judicial branch of government.
judicial.e: The judicial system ensures justice for all citizens.

junction.d: The act of joining, or the state of being joined.
junction.e: Turn left at the next junction to reach the hospital.

jurisdiction.d: The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply the law.
jurisdiction.e: The case falls under the jurisdiction of the high court.

justification.d: The alignment of text to the left margin (left justification), the right margin (right justification), or both margins (full justification).
justification.e: There is no justification for violence.

kidnap.d: To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.

kidney.d: An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.
kidney.e: The patient needs a kidney transplant.

kingdom.d: A realm, region, or conceptual space where something is dominant.

lad.d: A Jack the lad; a boyo.
lad.e: I think he reckons he's a bit of a lad.

landlord.d: A person who owns and rents land such as a house, apartment, or condo.
landlord.e: The landlord renovated the flat before the new tenants moved in.

landmark.d: An object that marks the boundary of a piece of land (usually a stone, or a tree).

lap.d: The loose part of a coat; the lower part of a garment that plays loosely; a skirt; an apron.
lap.e: The boy was sitting on his mother's lap.

large-scale.d: Large in amount, scope or extent.
large-scale.e: The large-scale festival attracted visitors from across the country.

laser.d: A device that produces a monochromatic, coherent beam of light.

latter.d: Relating to or being the second of two items.
latter.e: Between tea and coffee, I prefer the latter.

lawn.d: A piece of clothing made from lawn.
lawn.e: He mowed the lawn every weekend.

lawsuit.d: In civil law, a case where two or more people disagree and one or more of the parties take the case to a court for resolution.
lawsuit.e: The lawyer advised his client against filing a lawsuit as it would take a lot of time and money to resolve.

layout.d: A structured arrangement of items within certain limits.
layout.e: The layout of the new office is spacious and modern.

leak.d: To allow fluid or gas to pass through an opening that should be sealed.
leak.e: The faucet has been leaking since last month.

leap.d: To pass over by a leap or jump.
leap.e: to leap a wall or a ditch

legacy.d: Something inherited from a predecessor or the past.
legacy.e: John Muir left as his legacy an enduring spirit of respect for the environment.

legendary.d: Of or pertaining to a legend or to legends.
legendary.e: Sachin Tendulkar is a legendary cricketer.

legislation.d: Law which has been enacted by legislature or other governing body
legislation.e: The new legislation aims to protect consumer rights.

legislative.d: Making, or having the power to make, a law or laws; lawmaking
legislative.e: Although enormously influential in shaping the laws of the land, The House of Lords are not actually a legislative body.

legislature.d: A governmental body with the power to make, amend and repeal laws.
legislature.e: The state legislature passed the education reform bill.

legitimate.d: Conforming to known principles, or established or accepted rules or standards; valid.
legitimate.e: legitimate reasoning; a legitimate standard or method

lengthy.d: Having length; long and overextended, especially in time rather than dimension.
lengthy.e: She died last night after a lengthy illness.

lesbian.d: (of a woman) Homosexual, gay; preferring mostly or exclusively women as romantic or sexual partners.

lesser.d: Small in amount or number, having few members.

liable.d: Bound or obliged in law or equity; responsible; answerable.
liable.e: 1748. David Hume. Enquiries concerning the human understanding and concerning the principles of moral. London: Oxford University Press, 1973. § 34.

liberal.e: He was liberal with his compliments.

liberation.d: The act of liberating or the state of being liberated.
liberation.e: The liberation movement inspired generations of Indians.

liberty.d: The condition of being free from control or restrictions.
liberty.e: The army is here, your liberty is assured.

license.d: To give a formal (usually written) authorization.
license.e: It was decided to license Wikipedia under the GFDL.

lifelong.d: Extending for the entire duration of life.
lifelong.e: They were lifelong friends; they met in elementary school and ended their lives in the same rest home.

likelihood.d: The probability of a specified outcome; the chance of something happening; probability; the state or degree of being probable.
likelihood.e: In all likelihood the meeting will be cancelled.

limb.d: A major appendage of human or animal, used for locomotion (such as an arm, leg or wing).

line-up.d: Collectively, the members of a team.
line-up.e: The manager fielded his strongest lineup for the game against United.

linear.d: Made, or designed to be used, in a step-by-step, sequential manner.

linger.d: To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.
linger.e: The aroma of freshly cooked biryani lingered in the kitchen.

listing.d: The action of the verb to list.
listing.e: Aardvaark Plumbing is the first listing in Yellow Pages.

literacy.e: The literacy rate in India has improved significantly over the years.

liver.d: A large organ in the body that stores and metabolizes nutrients, destroys toxins and produces bile. It is responsible for thousands of biochemical reactions.
liver.e: Steve Jobs is a famous liver transplant recipient.

lobby.d: To attempt to influence (a public official or decision-maker) in favor of a specific opinion or cause.
lobby.e: For years, pro-life groups have continued to lobby hard for restrictions on abortion.

log.d: To make, to add an entry (or more) in a log or logbook.
log.e: to log the miles travelled by a ship

logic.d: Reasoning conducted according to strict principles of validity; a system of reasoning.

long-standing.d: Having been done for long enough time to become convention.
long-standing.e: Long-standing custom calls for referring to the town chairman as mayor, even though we don't have a mayor.

long-time.d: Having endured for a long period of time.
long-time.e: My longtime friend, since birth actually, called and gently broke the bad news to me.

loom.d: To appear indistinctly, eg. when seen on the horizon or through the murk.
loom.e: The clouds loomed over the mountains.

loop.d: A length of thread, line or rope that is doubled over to make an opening.
loop.e: Arches, loops, and whorls are patterns found in fingerprints.

loyalty.d: Faithfulness or devotion to some person, cause or nation.
loyalty.e: The company rewards employee loyalty with special bonuses.

machinery.d: The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.
machinery.e: The factory installed new machinery to increase production.

magical.e: The fireworks created a magical atmosphere in that beautiful summer night.

magistrate.d: A judicial officer with limited authority to administer and enforce the law. A magistrate's court may have jurisdiction in civil or criminal cases, or both.
magistrate.e: The magistrate heard the case in the district court.

magnitude.d: A logarithmic scale of brightness defined so that a difference of 5 magnitudes is a factor of 100.
magnitude.e: The earthquake measured seven on the magnitude scale.

mainland.d: The principal island of a group.
mainland.e: The ferry connects the island to the mainland.

mainstream.d: The principal current in a flow, such as a river or flow of air
mainstream.e: His ideas were well outside the mainstream, but he presented them intelligently, and we were impressed if not convinced.

maintenance.d: Actions performed to keep some machine or system functioning or in service.
maintenance.e: Regular maintenance keeps the car in good condition.

mandate.d: The authority to do something, as granted to a politician by the electorate.
mandate.e: The government received a strong mandate from the voters.

mandatory.d: Of, being or relating to a mandate.

manifest.d: To show plainly; to make to appear distinctly, usually to the mind; to put beyond question or doubt; to display; to exhibit.
manifest.e: His courage manifested itself through the look on his face.

manipulate.d: To move, arrange or operate something using the hands
manipulate.e: It is wrong to manipulate others for personal gain.

manipulation.d: The practice of manipulating or the state of being manipulated.
manipulation.e: The dealer's manipulations could have removed cards from the deck.

manuscript.d: A book, composition or any other document, written by hand (or manually typewritten), not mechanically reproduced.

marginal.d: Of, relating to, or located at or near a margin or edge; also figurative usages of location and margin (edge).
marginal.e: In recent years there has been an increase in violence against marginal groups.

marine.d: Belonging to or characteristic of the sea; existing or found in the sea; formed or produced by the sea.

marketplace.d: The space, actual or metaphorical, in which a market operates.
marketplace.e: Some high-street retailers were slow to enter the new digital marketplace of the Internet.

mask.d: A cover, or partial cover, for the face, used for disguise or protection.
mask.e: a dancer's mask; a fencer's mask; a ball player's mask

massacre.d: The killing of a considerable number (usually limited to people) where little or no resistance can be made, with indiscriminate violence, without necessity, and contrary to civilized norms.

mature.d: Brought to a state of complete readiness.

maximize.d: To make as large as possible
maximize.e: The farmer used modern techniques to maximize crop yield.

meaningful.e: I think we made a meaningful contribution to this project today.

meantime.d: The time spent waiting for another event; time in between.
meantime.e: I'll get started tomorrow but, in the meantime, let's see if we can get a few more opinions.

medieval.d: Of or relating to the Middle Ages, the period from approximately 500 to 1500 AD.

meditation.d: A musical theme treated in a meditative manner.
meditation.e: Daily meditation helps reduce anxiety and improve focus.

melody.d: Tune; sequence of notes that makes up a musical phrase
melody.e: The old Hindi song has a beautiful melody.

memoir.d: An autobiography; a book describing the personal experiences of an author.
memoir.e: When I retire, I'm going to write my memoirs.

mentor.d: A wise and trusted counselor or teacher

merchant.d: A person who traffics in commodities for profit.

mercy.d: Forgiveness or compassion, especially toward those less fortunate.
mercy.e: Have mercy on the poor and assist them if you can.

merely.e: She merely nodded in response to the question.

merge.e: Headquarters merged the operations of the three divisions.

merger.d: The act or process of merging two or more parts into a single unit.
merger.e: Club mergers reduced the number of teams by half

merit.d: A claim to commendation or a reward.
merit.e: For her good performance in the examination, her teacher gave her ten merits.

methodology.d: A collection of methods, practices, procedures and rules used by those who work in some field.

midst.d: A place in the middle of something; may be used of a literal or metaphorical location.
midst.e: In the midst of the crowd, she spotted her friend.

migration.d: An instance of moving to live in another place for a while.
migration.e: The migration of lead from a can to the food inside it can cause lead poisoning.

militant.d: Specifically, someone who supports the Trotskyist political view expressed in the newspaper Militant, or who engages in aggressive left-wing politics.
militant.e: The authorities arrested several militant suspects.

militia.d: The police in the Soviet Union and some related or successor states (e.g. modern Belarus).
militia.e: The local militia helped maintain order during the crisis.

mill.d: A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc.
mill.e: Pepper has a stronger flavor when it is ground straight from a mill.

minimal.d: The smallest possible amount, quantity, or degree.
minimal.e: The project was completed with minimal disruption.

minimize.d: To remove (a window) from the main display area, collapsing it to an icon or caption.
minimize.e: I didn't close anything, but I minimized all the windows so I could see the desktop.

mining.d: Any activity that extracts or undermines.
mining.e: His extensive mining for apparently statistically significant results made any of his results questionable.

ministry.d: Work of a spiritual or charitable nature.
ministry.e: the present ministry of the Holy Spirit

miracle.d: An event that appears inexplicable by the laws of nature and so is held to be supernatural in origin.
miracle.e: An example of a miracle associated with Muhammad is the splitting of the moon.

misery.d: Great unhappiness; extreme pain of body or mind; wretchedness; distress; woe.
misery.e: Ever since his wife left him you can see the misery on his face.

misleading.d: Deceptive or tending to mislead or create a false impression.

missile.d: Any object used as a weapon by being thrown or fired through the air, such as stone, arrow or bullet.
missile.e: The Rhodians, who used leaden bullets, were able to project their missiles twice as far as the Persian slingers, who used large stones.

mob.d: A large or disorderly group of people; especially one bent on riotous or destructive action.

mobility.d: The ability of a military unit to move or be transported to a new position.
mobility.e: Good public transport improves mobility for everyone.

mobilize.d: To assemble troops and their equipment in a coordinated fashion so as to be ready for war.
mobilize.e: The volunteers mobilized quickly to help the flood victims.

modification.d: A change to an organism as a result of its environment that is not transmissable to offspring.
modification.e: Due to his sunbathing, Jim's body experienced modifications: he got a tan.

momentum.d: Of a body in motion: the tendency of a body to maintain its inertial motion; the product of its mass and velocity.
momentum.e: The campaign gained momentum as the elections drew closer.

monk.d: A male member of a monastic order who has devoted his life for religious service.

monopoly.d: An exclusive control over the trade or production of a commodity or service through exclusive possession.
monopoly.e: A land monopoly renders its holder(s) nearly almighty in an agricultural society.

morality.d: A set of social rules, customs, traditions, beliefs, or practices which specify proper, acceptable forms of conduct.
morality.e: The story teaches children about morality and kindness.

motive.d: An idea or communication that makes one want to act, especially from spiritual sources; a divine prompting.
motive.e: No-one could understand why she had hidden the shovel; her motives were obscure at best.

motorist.d: One who drives a motor vehicle.
motorist.e: Motorists are advised to drive carefully on wet roads.

municipal.d: Of or pertaining to a municipality (a city or a corporation having the right of administering local government).
municipal.e: The municipal corporation manages waste collection in the city.

mutual.d: Having the same relationship, each to each other.

namely.e: Some of the students — namely Paul, Alice and Jake — seem to have trouble with geometry.

nationwide.e: There was a nationwide search on for the bankrobbers.

naval.d: Of or relating to ships in general.

neglect.d: To fail to care for or attend to something.
neglect.e: to neglect duty or business;  to neglect to pay debts

neighbouring.d: Situated or living nearby or adjacent to.
neighbouring.e: The neighbouring village is accessible by a narrow road.

nest.d: A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
nest.e: That nightclub is a nest of strange people!

newsletter.d: A periodically sent publication containing current events or the like, generally on a particular topic or geared toward a limited audience.
newsletter.e: The school sends a monthly newsletter to parents.

niche.d: A cavity, hollow, or recess, generally within the thickness of a wall, for a statue, bust, or other erect ornament. Hence, any similar position, literal or figurative.

noble.d: Having honorable qualities; having moral eminence and freedom from anything petty, mean or dubious in conduct and character.
noble.e: He is a noble man who would never put his family in jeopardy.

nominate.d: To entitle, confer a name upon.
nominate.e: 1658: the City of Norwich [...] was enlarged, builded and nominated by the Saxons. — Sir Thomas Browne, Urne-Burial (Penguin 2005, p. 12)

nomination.d: An act or instance of nominating.
nomination.e: She received a nomination for best actress at the film awards.

nominee.d: A person to whom the holder of a copyhold estate surrenders their interest.
nominee.e: The board approved the nominee for the chairman's position.

non-profit.d: Not seeking to produce a profit (a financial gain).

nonetheless.d: In spite of that; nevertheless; however.
nonetheless.e: The task was difficult; nonetheless, she completed it.

nonsense.d: Letters or words, in writing or speech, that have no meaning or pattern or seem to have no meaning.
nonsense.e: After my father had a stroke, every time he tried to talk, it sounded like nonsense.

noon.d: The corresponding time in the middle of the night; midnight.
noon.e: 1885, When night was at its noon I heard a voice chanting the Koran in sweetest accents — Sir Richard Burton, The Book of the Thousand Nights and One Night, Night 17:

notable.d: Worthy of note; remarkable; memorable; noted or distinguished.
notable.e: The scientist made several notable contributions to the field.

notably.d: (focus) As a pointed example; in a notable manner.
notably.e: Several states, notably Kerala and Tamil Nadu, have high literacy rates.

notify.e: Once a decision has been reached and notified to the parties it becomes binding.

notorious.d: Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.
notorious.e: The area was notorious for traffic jams during peak hours.

nursery.e: Commerce is the nursery of seamen.

objection.d: A statement expressing opposition, or a reason or cause for expressing opposition (generally followed by the adposition to).
objection.e: I have no objection to any person's religion.

oblige.d: To constrain someone by force or by social, moral or legal means.
oblige.e: I am obliged to report to the police station every week.

obsess.d: To dominate the thoughts of someone.
obsess.e: Thoughts of her obsess my every waking moment.

obsession.e: His obsession with cricket motivates him to practise daily.

occasional.d: Occurring or appearing irregularly from time to time, but not often.
occasional.e: He took an occasional glass of wine.

occurrence.d: An actual instance when a situation occurs; an event or happening.
occurrence.e: Such incidents are a rare occurrence in this neighbourhood.

odds.d: The ratio of the probability of an event happening to that of it not happening.
odds.e: I'd say the odds are strongly in favor of the sun rising tomorrow morning.

offering.d: The act by which something is offered.

offspring.d: Anything produced; the result of an entity's efforts.
offspring.e: Artists often treasure their works as their immortal offspring.

operational.d: Of or relating to operations, especially military operations.
operational.e: The new metro line is now fully operational.

optical.d: Designed to assist or enhance sight
optical.e: A microscope is an optical instrument.

optimism.d: A tendency to expect the best, or at least, a favourable outcome
optimism.e: I love her youth, her beauty and above all her optimism that everything will turn out fine.

organizational.d: Relating to the action of organizing something.

orientation.d: The designation of a parametrised curve as "positively" or "negatively" oriented (or "nonorientable"); the analogous description of a surface or hypersurface.
orientation.e: The homing instinct in pigeons is an example of orientation.

originate.d: To come into existence; to have origin or beginning; to spring, be derived (from, with).
originate.e: The scheme originated with the governor and council.

outbreak.d: An outburst or sudden eruption, especially of violence and mischief.
outbreak.e: There has been an outbreak of broken windows in the street.

outlet.d: A vent or similar passage to allow the escape of something.
outlet.e: The factory outlet offers products at discounted prices.

outlook.d: A place from which something can be viewed.
